Titles: What a waste of time and money
Review: "We are sorry for the extended delay. We will answer your call as soon as we can" This is all I get from ABC HealthCare. Along with what they call 'technical difficulties' with the app. When they can't help, which is standard operating procedure, they say "see your GP". The main reason for choosing ABC HealthCare and paying a very high premium because of my age was to save time and get prompt assistance. My experience amounts to endless phone calls, relentless excuses and summarily appalling service. I will be cancelling my policy and seeking another private health insurance provider because at this moment I cannot imagine any other company could be worse than this.

* UPDATE *  Over a week after this review, asking for a call, yesterday I received 4 calls from ABC HealthCare. Each one had *no one* there! I cannot emphasise how painful this is to try and deal with your organisation.  I now urgently need to organise treatment.  Please call me and stop using automated telephony which is clearly failing!  Thank you.  * UPDATE *  Dear ABC HealthCare, I refuse to write bad reviews however in your case, it is thoroughly deserved! I am writing this to get action, I cannot spend another 40 mins on the phone stuck in your system. When I do get through, in the quiet period in the evening you say the right team only has one person and when they eventually call me back days later, they can’t hear me and I’m in a quiet location. I get cut off.  You cancelled my account for no reason last year. The letter said ‘sorry my to see you go’. I have been with you since 2005/6.  It took 2 complaint letters to get a response back 7 months later, claiming my direct debit had been cancelled, yet you never told me of this. I never cancelled it. You also insist in charging me for 9 months+ for the time I could not use your services due to *your* cancellation in error.  I want to reinstate my account urgently.  You have my name, please call me.  Thank you.  Darren.

Titles: Should take sick people into consideration
Review: - The system assumes the individual is not very sick and can afford to waste a hour on the phone to secure authorisation numbers. - MRI should be included in the first authorisation as some conditions can't be discovered with a blood test or scans.
100%     Efficiency

Titles: 2 months to get an appointment mid march
Review: 2 months to get an appointment mid march

Titles: A call with regards to mental health…
Review: A call with regards to mental health for a family member and the person also mentioned that if i was suffering due to the family member not being well, he would recommend me to get some help.  I appreciated that concern a lot as he was spot on.
A great professional and calming experience. Felt at ease and cared for. All staff friendly and welcoming
A minimum of formality. Very helpful and sympathetic. Very efficient.

Titles: A really easy company to deal with
Review: A really easy company to deal with, no fuss, polite and knowledgeable people on the end of the telephones.

Titles: A really suburb company that evaluates…
Review: A really suburb company that evaluates claims promptly & either responds or pay out without undue delay!

Titles: A very helpful voice on the end of the…
Review: A very helpful voice on the end of the phone called Chris  - he made the call easy and very efficient.  I was emailed and sent a text whilst still on the call with him with the relevant information I needed to continue my journey with ABC HealthCare.  My one note is that some of the consultants don't work at the hospital lists provided but I suspect that is up to them to inform ABC HealthCare so information can be updated!

AVOID!!! Awful company to deal with. I have taken out PMI with them and upon trying to set up the ABC HealthCare Blua app, which allows you access to your services, there was a technical fault and I had to call the technical team. I called every day for around 2 weeks and each time I was hold for more than 30 minutes, so I hung up. Finally got through this morning to be told it's an I.T issue and they could not give me a time frame when it would be fixed! I've contacted them regarding some other issues prior to this and nobody seems to know what they are doing when they answer the phone or they just want to palm you off to another department as quick as they can. They are honestly one of the worst companies I've ever dealt with and I would never recommend anyone to join them.
Able to talk to someone, the person being friendly and helpful, trying to address the issue immediately and effectively.

Absolute crap. Absurdly hard to navigate app ABC HealthCare Touch that makes you open a separate app Babylon. Try to schedule a call consultation, takes all your info..confirms... then no one ever calls. You try again and you're asked to pay....You give up and call...but wait...their phone lines are limited or closed depending on the day. Forget Sunday.  And when they are opened prepare to wait a LONG LONG time.   We pay for this!???  Why is it impossible to get ABC HealthCare to answer the phone? - the worst customer service.

Absolute rubbish service, call 7 times and each time was given a different answer to the same question. ABC HealthCare keep no proper records of conversations and claim that recordings of calls go missing. They don't reply to emails to confirm a claim is valid to avoid paying up once you have had treatment, their colors change when you make a legit claim. Call waiting times are terrible and IVR option are ambiguous, wasting time being passed on from one department to another. A total shambles !

Titles: I had to wait on the phone Fir over an hour to get any response and that too after I had spoken to the agent the day or so before and I was not clear at all about the conditions and did not get a mess
Review: Absolutely bad sxoerience Had two hours esit on the phone on two occasions that I called ABC HealthCare

Absolutely disgusting service I pay every month I wanted a blood test for a number of symptoms but apparently its not good enough I need a referral from the doctor!!! If I could see the doctor I wouldn't need private healthcare !!!!

Titles: First class service from start to finish
Review: Absolutely fantastic service from dealing with the pre authorisation to having completed the procedure, everyone involved was professional, friendly and wanting to please.

Absolutely shocking. I’ve been a member of ABC HealthCare for several years but this month (May 2023) decided to use them for a medical concern for the first time. I didn’t realise that one has to do all the leg work! Been trying to get an appointment for relatively minor surgery ALL DAY. They don’t do anything! One even has to open further accounts just to send and receive emails. Forget about getting though on phone unless to want to wait 30 mins. The process is a massive complicated mess and you’ll get no help. One has to download an app (log in and password again) that has got nothing to do with ABC HealthCare! When I finally got through to a doctor’s secretary on this app I she told me she couldn’t do anything without an authorisation code. ABC HealthCare never mentioned anything about that. I told her I’d be better off going to my garage and sorting through some tools to do the surgery myself! Save yourself a massive job and just wait for NHS.

Titles: Fix your Network. No one accepts any referral.
Review: Absurdly long wait times in call. The folks in the calls are nice though, nothing at all against them.  The list of pre-approved doctors never accepted any referrals. I went through at last 30-40 different doctors but just nothing. Fix your network. Waste of a year worth of premium paid into this, that too for the whole family.

After a rocky start trying to understand exactly what I'm covered for and getting bogged down with jargon from ABC HealthCare, the process is now much clearer and has been relatively easy to arrange pre-authorisations.

After an initial set-up to get my dental plan to upload with my account .  Everything was fine.  I was unsure if a broken tooth was an emergency or not. A definition guide for an emergency would be usefull
After difficulty getting through to someone with working systems to book an appointment, the service provided by the provider I did choose has been great.

After joining ABC HealthCare health insurance for more than three weeks, I still haven't managed to register online after seeking help through live chat once and telephone calls twice. My complaints and the indication of my intention of leaving a negative review here failed accelerate their responding time which is up to three weeks! That would mean potentially I won't get their online service for more than a month after I joined. This is not acceptable.

Titles: More than doubled premium because of a claim less in value than my excess
Review: After just one year more than doubling my premium on basis I had a claim, it was the most minor of claims which totalled less than my excess ie it cost ABC HealthCare nothing. Tried to communicate via chat on their website, waited about 20 mins only to get message saying it ‘couldn’t connect’.

Titles: Great Service From Contact to starting treatment.
Review: After my initial contact we ABC HealthCare I was given the okay to contact my chosen consultant for my consultation. From there he dealt with ABC HealthCare who okayed my blood cancer treatment of which I am now underway with cannot fault the very high service I have received. Which has now given me peace of mind.

Titles: After my initial experience with ABC HealthCare
Review: After my initial experience with ABC HealthCare, being left on hold for 27 minutes on the helpline, my last call was quickly answered and dealt with effectively

Titles: After the initial time taken to get an…
Review: After the initial time taken to get an authorisation code, everything went really smoothly with the insurance process

Titles: ABC HealthCare  In Touch app doesn't work for me.
Review: After the now obligatory 30 minute wait for them to answer the phone, I gave up and asked for help via their complaints page:- "Following receipt today of a letter from ABC HealthCare asking me to download and enrol ABC HealthCare Touch, I tried to do so.  Having completed the forms and being asked to log in with the details entered, I am repeatedly told “Invalid Details”. Tried the digital helpline, placed in a queue due to “unexpectedly call volumes”, Really? Virtual chat: told they couldn’t help me. Can you help?" Since then I've heard nothing. I've been a member/customer for over 20 years and witnessed a once great company slide ever further down the customer service slide. I would have left years ago, but the health insurance business has a clear model, "We don't accept any pre-existing conditions", so you're stuck with your current provider.

After years of paying in I finally had to use ABC HealthCare and was pleasantly surprised. My wife and I had considered cancelling ABC HealthCare as it has started to really cost as we’ve got older. However I recently found a lump and was very glad I hadn’t. I phoned my GP surgery as soon as I found said lump and was told it’d be a week or more before I could see a doctor as it wasn’t urgent. It felt pretty urgent to me! My wife suggested we tried the ABC HealthCare GP service while I waited, after all we pay for it but had never used it. I had a video consultation within the hour of using the ABC HealthCare app and the GP immediately put my mind at ease by diagnosing an ingunial hernia and recommend that I saw a surgeon. An hour later ABC HealthCare had given me the details for a local hospital and surgeon and 6 weeks later I was in and out the same day for surgery. The hospital a Spire was fine, a little tired maybe but the important bit the theatre looked brand new to me? The last time I was in an operating theatre was 35 years ago and to say things have moved on is putting it mildly. The care from start to finish was also first class. I’m a massive supporter of the NHS but at the moment if you can afford it I’d highly recommend considering ABC HealthCare. The way I look at it, I also freed up an NHS space for someone less fortunate than myself.
Again, a very quick and easy claims submittal process with a fast turnaround and payment of the claim

Agents are always helpful and friendly. They are knowledgeable and have always handled my queries quickly and accurately. Provision of service has been quick and efficient. Communications are always good including emails and text messages.

Titles: Agents on the phone are really helpful…
Review: Agents on the phone are really helpful and friendly, I have 2 local clinics within a 30 min drive. The doctors are also friendly, knowledgeable and really seem to want to help you reach a solution

Titles: All dealings with burps ABC HealthCare have been…
Review: All dealings with ABC HealthCare have been very painless, informative and above my expectation. Communication is quick and clear, phone calls and availability of appointments easy to deal with and support has been excellent.
All in all, it is fairly straightforward.
All round outstanding service, prompt approval to specialists & treatments, thankyou
All staff that I dealt with just put my mind at ease and talked me through everything with ease.

Titles: All staff were very helpfull and the…
Review: All staff were very helpfull and the doctors explained all the procedures.

All the staff at the Hessle hospital were always professional and really lovely and helpful.  Really put me at ease. My consultant was the best I am so pleased of my choice. Fantastic experience all round. The facilities were also great 👍

Titles: Unmanned Call Centre??? Seems like it
Review: Almost in a positon to giving up trying to contact them, on hold for what seems to be an eternity....cannot wait longer than 15mins....but looking at the other reviews it will be longer :-(

Always friendly and helpful.

Always nice representitives. Always ready to help. Only the best experiences with ABC HealthCare.
Amazing service from ABC HealthCare. Received payment quickly and good you can still use your NHS and claim back
Amazing service from the first telephone all right through to the treatment. Outstanding!
Amazing staff at BMI Meriden who explained everything, were diligent and patient and really cared about the people they were helping. Nothing was too much trouble. Fabulous!

Titles: Amazing support and so helpful with our…
Review: Amazing support and so helpful with our needs.

Titles: Amy Wakeman has been wonderful to deal…
Review: Amy Wakeman has been wonderful to deal with

An absolutely shocking experience. It took me over 8 weeks to get through on the ABC HealthCare helpline number. Apparently it is usual to have to wait in excess of 60 minutes to speak to someone. I raised a formal complaint with telephone screen shots of all my failed attempts, in excess of 4 hours and 8 calls by the time I started collating. Friends/colleagues with other providers had no such issues. Luckily NHS stepped in and provided excellent swift CT scans etc etc saving ABC HealthCare around £10,000. I raised a formal complaint which ABC HealthCare apologised for and sent me £150 compensation. Which I will politely say was insulting. Especailly as I then had to pay the excess, despite saving them thousands. Never ever use ABC HealthCare!!!

Titles: More excessive ABC HealthCare price increases
Review: And it is time for renewal of my policy. After significant increases last year, this year ABC HealthCare choose to increase the price by a huge amount - 27%. We are now in the unaffordable range. Yet again all that is stated is that there can be various factors explaining why there is an increase including if you have had the audacity to actually use the policy. It feel like ABC HealthCare are trying to hide the reasons for the increase rather than explaining exactly what the increase covers - for instance rising cost x amount, that you have used your policy y, your age z.  Add on top of that how difficult it is to actually get through to anyone in case you need to contact them. Time to look to other providers recommended by others.

Titles: And yet again, I’m let down
Review: And yet again, I’m let down by ABC HealthCare when in a vulnerable position mentally, then ignored by the ‘directors’.  I saw the FIFTH different psychiatrist thanks to ABC HealthCare, arranged a treatment and then they come up with some stupid reason as to why they ‘cant’ pay for it.   Terrible service, cannot rely on them at all.   Take some responsibility for your customers.   Genuinely heartbroken this is the outcome of someone whom tries so hard to get better.
Appalling AP for Dental plan..which forces you to find a computer to do a claim. Appalling customer services when you try to ring up flagging the problem--left holding for half an hour

Appalling customer service, poorly trained staff. Basic courtesy goes a long way in my book. They don't follow through on what they tell you, talk over you while you try and explain and couldn't give give a flying fig when you cancel.  Shop around trust me its worth it, year on year rises, service gets worse thankfully i'm not actually ill trying to get something out of them. Policy cancelled gone to Aviva just have to hope its better.

Appalling customer service. The worst I have ever encountered. And trying to complain is futile. They ignore emails. Customers are treated with contempt. They make showy promises of excellence but what they provide is truly dreadful

Titles: It should be possible to increase an excess
Review: Apparently it's not possible to reduce my premium by increasing the excess, simply because I joined as part of a company discounted scheme. But I suggest in reality it just needs someone to be bothered to renegotiate to enable higher excesses to be chosen. With inflation high, the real value of a capped excess is surely falling.
Appointment availability was great and staff were helpful
Appointment was on time, staff were friendly and the technician excellent.
Appointments were so easy, experience of the Doctors meant I was seen quickly and my health issue was dealt with

As I have a ABC HealthCare Dentist, payment is done straight away which is easy,confirmation of treatment appears on the app very quickly. Very happy with the professional service and the plan I chose

As a long-time customer of ABC HealthCare, I have always relied on their reputation for providing quality health insurance services. However, my recent experience with their claim process has left me deeply dissatisfied and frustrated. The whole ordeal has been nothing short of a nightmare, and I feel compelled to share my grievances to caution others about what they might encounter. The lack of communication was further exacerbated by the unresponsiveness of their customer service representatives. Each time I attempted to inquire about the status of my claim, I was met given different responses and empty promises.  Moreover, the claim process itself seemed designed to be intentionally complex and confusing. The communication channels are limited and difficult and time-consuming.  To make matters worse, ABC HealthCare's decision on my claim seemed arbitrary and unjust. After waiting for weeks on end, they ultimately denied my claim with no credible reason. It felt as if they were intentionally trying to find reasons to reject about £100 claim rather than provide the support they advertise.  Overall, my recent experience with ABC HealthCare's claim process has been disappointing and has left me questioning my decision to remain their customer. Their lack of transparency, and seemingly deliberate complexity of the reason behind rejection demonstrated a complete disregard for policyholders' well-being and satisfaction. Until ABC HealthCare rectify the issue, I cannot recommend ABC HealthCare as a reliable health insurance provider.

Titles: Bad start to a new membership
Review: As a new (senior) member I was not given empathetic support when I struggled to access and understand the system. I was also given out of date information about providers. I submitted a complaint in early May and was promised a reply in three weeks. It’s now over a month and I’m still waiting.

Titles: As always ABC HealthCare was excellent
Review: As always ABC HealthCare was excellent, every thing on their part went very smoothly

Titles: Use another provider - do not use ABC HealthCare if you care about your health and cost
Review: As soon as you get a long term health issue, they punish you with extortionate premiums.  Avoid - use Axa or another provider every time.  ABC HealthCare need to be broken up by the regulator. They are just wolves dressed as sheep.  I had cancer in my 20s, they now charge me ludicrous amounts (even though I now don’t claim).  They also increase prices that can’t be justified based on internal inflation numbers - and also have arbitrary changes in price for address changes.  They are not out to help patients or doctors or HCPs. They just are out to pay themselves big salaries and have big unnecessary (and mostly useless) teams.

Titles: Calling your ABC HealthCare insurance company !
Review: At times it can be difficult  getting through on the telephone  and you have  to hold on for several minutes ,but when you do get through the person on the call is courteous,  friendly ,puts you at ease and helps you navigate  through the information  requirements. Once that is sorted you are then given any authorisation  codes you require and an explanation  of what they actually cover. All in all, friendly and knowledgeable

Automated call service is dire, no obvious choice if you want to actually speak to somebody. Lots of options but nothing of use to me. Kept waiting for 12mins before I got through to somebody, they then kept making out my line was bad and couldn't hear me. Eventually did an online chat to get my questions answered.
Aways prompt when answering calls. staff friendly. Never have any issues when i need authorisation codes. Used ABC HealthCare a lot over past few years and they have been great every single time.
Awful customer service advised I would get a call back the next day I’ve been waiting over 2 weeks. Tried to chat but got cut off. Overall just an awful experience

Awful. Had to fight tooth and nail, getting multiple letters from my physio, to try and get cover for a new type of treatment that they said they do cover.   I previously had insurance with AXA, who were 20x more compassionate, understanding, and never tried to deny my claims. Really regret being with ABC HealthCare.

Awful. Tried for a week to get through on the phones. Spent over 5 hours waiting in total on different days.  I ended up toughing it out for 90 minutes and finally got though. Good service when you get through but awful wait times and not good enough for the cost. I will be reporting this to my business and suggesting that they find another provider.

Titles: Thanking Dylan Somaiya for his help
Review: ABC HealthCare Insurance is brilliant and easy to use for my family. I have been especially impressed by the level of customer service and no one exemplifies that better than Dylan Somaiya in ABC HealthCare sales.Dylan has been incredibly efficient and helpful in guiding me at every step to get the best ABC HealthCare coverage for my family. He has patiently taken the time to explain the various plans and always been responsive in coming back as scheduled. Further, he has expertly answered all my questions and followed up each time. A real pleasure to work with Dylan. Special thanks for his help - he is a tremendous credit to ABC HealthCare.

Titles: Not interested in helping people who are ill
Review: ABC HealthCare are only interested in helping healthy people they can make some money from.  Was told that they cannot cover my pre-existing Crohn's disease - obviously I am not going to be a money spinner.  "Your wellbeing, on your terms. This is health." Absolute rubbish. They don't care whatsoever about individual people with individual concerns.

ABC HealthCare customer for 20 years plus.Claims are always met with the  " not included on your policy". Refused physio treatment because I had not been an inpatient etc , etc. My wife's treatment for breast cancer was brilliant under NHS. When I applied for " NHS Credit" was informed that for an operation credit would be £250 !! Where can I get a consultant, anaesthetist , theatre ,nurses for even 10 times that sum ? I e-m CEO and HR at ABC HealthCare -- no reply. Telephoned to ask why  I had no reply and informed them I would withhold payment until reply received . Only result was that my policy was cancelled . Good riddance.

Titles: ABC HealthCare desperately need to update their…
Review: ABC HealthCare desperately need to update their pay out costs and there is quite often a shortfall which you the patient then have to pay

Titles: ABC HealthCare expect the patient to do a lot of…
Review: ABC HealthCare expect the patient to do a lot of their work (like getting 'procedure codes', GP to fill in forms (within impossible timescales), refuse to liaise with the private hospital saying they have to speak to the patient in person. I ended having to pay out myself (although I will be reimbursed in time) to get the operation done quickly. They refused to reimburse the GP fee (£40). I wouldn't recommend ABC HealthCare, It was a constant battle with them, and further stress I didn't need.

Titles: ABC HealthCare gave me access to a range of…
Review: ABC HealthCare gave me access to a range of experts to discuss my situation. The service I received was efficient and I only had to attend my appointment with very little admin. Was seen to promptly and service was excellent.
ABC HealthCare got back to me quickly to say that they had paid my dental claim to my bank account.
ABC HealthCare has always been helpful and very patient with me. I honestly don’t know what I would have done without my ABC HealthCare policy! They’ve helped me get through a lot.

Titles: great customer service, good PM offer, smooth process of claiming consultations
Review: ABC HealthCare has been really helpful in allowing me to pro-actively seek advice and examinations, where NHS would not even consider my worries. I've received mental health support as well as one-off consultations for different routine examinations such as gynaecology. I am very grateful my employer pays for my ABC HealthCare insurance and very grateful to the customer team for helping fast with my queries.

ABC HealthCare have been incredibly supportive and have dealt with all the queries, concerns etc  I have had over the past two years with kindness and efficiency. I cannot rate them highly enough and have been recommending ABC HealthCare to all my family and friends
ABC HealthCare representative was very friendly and understanding,

Titles: No follow-up from ABC HealthCare after they responded to my original review
Review: ABC HealthCare responded to my last 1-star review saying that they would get Customer Relations to get in contact with me to help me raise a complaint - I haven’t heard from anyone.  Unfortunately another broken promise.  Why go through the trouble to communicate someone will be in touch and they don’t - you just make the matter even worse.  Maybe they thought that posting a response to my review on TrustPilot will counteract the negativity of my review.

Titles: ABC HealthCare saved me 60 pounds per month…
Review: ABC HealthCare saved me 60 pounds per month thanks to Insured health

Titles: service levels must match marketing hype
Review: ABC HealthCare sell their expertise in cancer care. However I wonder how many cancer sufferers can afford an annual hike in costs of 38%. Most will be priced out of care, and with a pre existing condition will be unable to seek competitively priced care elsewhere. As a premium care provider, it is also inappropriate not to cover the cost of the best surgeons, so clients either accept surgeons who are not in the top tier, or they have to pay for it themselves. All this seems a little unethical. The service is not matching the marketing.

Titles: ABC HealthCare support during my cancer treatment…
Review: ABC HealthCare support during my cancer treatment has been first rate - thank you.
ABC HealthCare was very fast in agreeing my request for consultation, surgery and follow up appointment.  The private suite at the Essex Cardiothoracic Centre was excellent in all respects.

Titles: ABC HealthCare, making your life HARD
Review: ABC HealthCare, for when you need a Health Insurance Provider that just doesn't give a damn about you! Having just had a heart attack and being with ABC HealthCare for decades I thought it would be easy to navigate the many, many avenues of the medical profession that I now need to do. But NO, from the very start ABC HealthCare had, and continues, to make sorting out all issues as hard as they can be. It seems that they are going out of the way to be difficult and throw up obstacles.
ABC HealthCare’s response and support throughout my illness has been first class. I would not have coped without them.

Titles: Be prepared for a long wait
Review: Be prepared for a long wait. I contacted ABC HealthCare for medical assistance on 21 April. They need verification from my GP that is wasn't pre-existing condition. I didn't get a response from BUPE until the 26 June which was text message telling me to call them. it took several attempts and lots of waiting on the line, listening to and responding to endless automated messages before I finally got through to human being.

Titles: Shambolic organisation and "computer says no" attitude
Review: Been paying for ABC HealthCare insurance for 7 years through workplace scheme, added my newborn baby recently and needed to get his tongue tie sorted as it is causing the whole family a lot of stress and seep deprivation. I obtained my referral and called up and was provided with 3 consultants "in the local area" who could provide the service. I booked an appointment with 1 at Kings College Hospital for a weeks' time (tomorrow now). I call up today just to be sure that my pre-authorisations are correct, and am told that actually whilst the surgeon himself can provide the service, they won't let him do it at that hospital. The procedure doesn't even need to be carried out by a doctor it is so simple.  Speak to the surgeon again directly and he's only ever worked at this hospital and has multiple other ABC HealthCare insured familys having the procedure done this week. So either,  how is he ABC HealthCare approved in the first place or why am I being denied access to local private healthcare when others aren't? And why was I provided with his details? I ask to speak to a manager and am told they will call me back some time in the next 48 hours to resolve the issue, when my appointment is the next evening. SO what am I supposed to do?! I then have to spend THREE HOURS  on the phone trying to register a complaint and get the issue sorted. During this time I am told me issue has been marked as "urgent". WHat does that mean? That I will be called back some time in the next 48 hours. So the same as not urgent. Eventually I am told that I have to cancel my current appointment at a nearby hospital (that some but not all ABC HealthCare customers are allowed to use apparently) and book a whole new appointment, at some undefined time in the future, at one of a number of hospitals the other side of London. The whole experience was deeply stressful and frustrating and will result in us having to put up with the difficulties this is causing us for another whole week and my partner having to take our son across London to have the procedure done, due to ABC HealthCare's ridiculous administrative. Why is someone listed as a "ABC HealthCare Platinum Consultant" and "Fee Assured" on ABC HealthCare's own site, if he can't actually do anything bar tell you that your child needs a procedure. I am so bitterly disappointed with this experience it has forever tarnished my view of a company I had been led to believe provided a good service. Do better.

Titles: Been with ABC HealthCare for a couple of years…
Review: Been with ABC HealthCare for a couple of years and paid about £3600 in subscriptions . Recently I’ve had a very painful elbow and called them for advice as to what to do ,they didn’t give me any advise and gave a clear indication that they didn’t give a damn as to my plight . I’ve cancelled my subscription and advised my friends and colleagues as to my situation and experience . Read the recent reviews and judge for yourself. ABC HealthCare is only a third party and have no say in the outcome of any situation. Keep your money safely in your pocket before committing.  KB   London
Being able to request treatment online without a Dr referral has made a good process much much better.

Best move I ever made, I have ad to use Bua twice now and I have to say both experiences were excellent plus it's the everyday reassurance that if the worst was to happen ABC HealthCare would have my back.
Booking an appointment was easy, great communication, making a claim is very simple and resolved quickly.
Both ABC HealthCare Insurance and their associated practices are amazing! Insurance short and sweet and the practice just... Five stars!

Brilliant care from the start. Can always get through to the phone lines promptly and referrals are quick and easy to organise. All the doctors/ clinicians I've seen have been helpful. The best thing about my current employer is that they offer ABC HealthCare.
Brilliant customer service

Brilliant support provided for my recent illness which helped me to recover more quickly than I would have done on the NHS (I’m still on the NHS waiting list!). I’m so thankful that I have this cover.

Broadly satisfied with wide choice of approved consultants and hospitals. However disappointed that the outpatients cover is limited to the same £1,000 for a very long time despite cost of consultations and treatments increasing

Broke my leg and wasn't in need of any hassle to get consultant and physio organised but ABC HealthCare sorted quickly and efficiently. Both appointments have been easy to arrange and staff have been great at Park Hill
ABC HealthCare are always quick to answer the phone and efficient with their help. Their customer service representatives are always very kind on the phone as well.

ABC HealthCare care nothing except for themselves and their profits. The local vet would have more sympathy. Is this the publicity that those in charge of ABC HealthCare want? ABC HealthCare state 'the service you deserve' - quite clearly I deserve nothing despite paying premiums - please read the history of my ABC HealthCare reviews showing what an uncaring and shambolic organisation ABC HealthCare is. PS - 'reach out' means ABC HealthCare will knock you down while you have medical problems

Titles: Getting through to them is absolutely hopeless.
Review: ABC HealthCare has always been great in terms of authorising treatment and explaining what is and isn't covered. However, recently it has been absolutely hopeless trying to get through. The message on the phone says up to half an hour but usually it is much longer. Worse still is the online chat. The dreadful bot tells you the wait time is 15 mins. It then counts down until it reaches 0. It then tells you 'this is taking longer than expected' before, finally, saying 'Thanks for chatting with us'.

ABC HealthCare have an appalling phone service and really their directors and managers ought to be ashamed.  They take hundreds of pounds each month from their members, (my husband and I spend nearly £10,000 a year agghh!)  yet they have allowed their phone service to go from bad to abysmal. This morning it was a one-hour wait, wasting my time.  I've been with ABC HealthCare for forty years, needing very little medical help, but in the last couple of years I needed a knee op and then a shoulder op.  So I have had to ring them several times over that period.  Their phone service has been appalling every single time.  Some days you just have to give up and leave it for another day. The phone message has ALWAYS said they "are experiencing a very high level of calls" so this isn't anything new that has taken them by surprise. Now they say they are training new call handlers. I just spoke to one (after a one hour wait) and he knew very little about ABC HealthCare.  He didn't know whether there was a specialist orthopaedic dept (there used to be), couldn't find a consultant even though I found him on the ABC HealthCare website, and couldn't answer a basic question about our excess.  I have no idea who is running this organisation but it's about time they got their act together given the outrageously high fees they charge. If you are looking for private medical insurance this is not a good time to choose ABC HealthCare. Wait until they can cope properly with their member enquiries.

Titles: Extremely slow to respond to emails and difficult to get the ball rolling
Review: ABC HealthCare have been fine for my dental work, but my medical work has been a different story. I've been trying to organize therapy for mental health and they advised I fill out some online form. They then took a few weeks to respond to tell me I needed to get a GP referral. I get the referral, and I've been waiting 4 working days for a response so far, not expecting a response for another week or two I'd expect. Not sure why they state they respond within a few working days when this is just not accurate?  Edit RE response from ABC HealthCare, I DID send an email and even got a "we aim to respond in X days" response from them, so it's false that they never received my message or their systems are just broken.

Looks like I will have to use the Ombudsman !

Titles: Multiple problems but the main issue is…
Review: Communications Last month (April) when trying to contact ABC HealthCare i discovered multiple problems but the main issue is communication. My first attempt at contacting ABC HealthCare was when i called and waited 55mins without it being answered . So why waiting I looked on line and saw a different number so I thought that the original number called might not be the best number so I rang off and tried the other number but that was no better. I also tryied their online system and that was telling me that it would be at least 45 mins. After another hour wait on the phone I did get to speak with a call handler. I suggested 2 consultants ABC HealthCare said that since I came up with 2 then they would provide 2 alternative consultants which they did by email but these were consultants 100 miles away. I tried to email ABC HealthCare straight back to say that these consultants were not local but it went to a no reply box. So I ventured back to the phone because i could not find a general email address . Another 45 mins of my life that I will not be getting back. I have complained directly to ABC HealthCare and I have been told that they are bringing in an additional 60 call handlers since they are aware of the problem of long wait times on the phone.  Update following comments  Thank you for confirming that there have been serious issues with waiting times. It is interesting that there appears ro be little problems in contacting ABC HealthCare sales team and sending the sales team a email which was quickly responded to.  Maybe it would be good if there was an email address which members could use as well in case members simply wanted to send a message? Rather than take the lottery of trying to use a phone?

Customer service is poor. I obtained authorisation for a consultant visit, mri and X-ray and then authorisation for full hip replacement through my husbands policy. In the meantime I got my own policy through work. ABC HealthCare system automatically put the claims against my policy even though I had never request authorisation for any treatment on my own policy and certainly did not quote my policy number at the hospital. Recieved several letters from ABC HealthCare saying they wouldn’t pay for consultation etc. all while waiting for a total hip replacement. Spend an hour on phone to a person at ABC HealthCare explaining that their system had allocated invoices to wrong policy. Person was very rude and could not understand my severe concern over liability for payment because their own system messed up! I was told claims team would look into in within 5 days. Nothing happened. Consultant team were also chasing me for payment because ABC HealthCare system couldn’t accept invoice cause it had been allocated by ABC HealthCare, not me, against wrong policy. I had to contact ABC HealthCare again, first by live chat that was absolutely hopeless and did not even answer my question and then through live chat with a claims team member. Every time having to explain same story and every time with little empathy and claims of them having no record of previous conversations and calls. The ability to download transcript of chat did not work either! I am 46 and to have a total hip replacement is very stressful it was made even more of a worrying time by ABC HealthCares Incompetence and inability to action what was agreed first time once I’d alerted them to their problem. In the end it took three calls of over an hour each to sort a problem their own system created because I never told anyone my own policy number. I feel aggrevied at having to spend my time sorting their problem out and need to report to you that the customer service and attitude of the people involved was very poor.

Thank you also for providing me with valuable oral hygiene tips and guidance to maintain a healthy smile. Your advice will undoubtedly help me improve my dental health in the long run. Titles: Hassle-free easy to follow and a great…
Review: Hassle-free easy to follow and a great service

Have been a paying customer for 6 months. Now that I need something I'm getting to see how bad this is.  There are about 5 different apps for things, all with really unhelpful names that dont say what they do. First you have to figure out if you need "ABC HealthCare Blue" or "Babylon" or "ABC HealthCare Touch" for the thing you need.  My login for Babylon works, but not for the other ones. I'm promted to use the live chat which tells the times live chat works followed by "live chat is not available" despite it being the middle of the day during live chat hours.  Trying to register I get a generic "we cant register you call this number" and when I call said number I have options all related to buying a new policy which aren't relevant to me.  Eventually spoke to someone in customer service today who was super rude. Literally shouting over the top of me and cutting me out, not listening to a work I say just repeating boilerplate.  Anyways. After all this I now need to get an NHS GP appointment to fill out some new forms to prove its not an existing condition, then once I've managed that I have to wait 5 working days for ABC HealthCare to process it, despite me already filling out pages and pages of this to start with, and having access to my complete medical record.  I expect they're just hoping people will pay for the treatment themselves and they won't get round to this. This may well be the case with me.  Really wish I hadn't bothered with this lot.

Have been waiting a surgical procedure with the NHS since January 2022.  Contacted ABC HealthCare to discuss my options through them and had a consultation appointment within 5 days, follow up procedure within a week and have booked surgery at my convenience. Very quick, easy and simple process to book and frees up the NHS to help somebody else, which has to be a win-win.

Have been with ABC HealthCare before and in the past it was always a first rate service.  This time round I've found them far too quick to try and exclude conditions if they can in any way be remotely linked to something pre-existing.  Have also spent many hours on the phone trying to get through - apparently call volumes are permanently high which to me just means they don't employ enough staff.

Have unfortunately spent a lot of time on the phone to ABC HealthCare tihs year due to my partner's cancer treatment. Sometimes calls are answered in a minute or two, other times I have ben on hold for over an hour, which is rather frustrating, even when calling their dedicated cancert support line. Quality of customer support varies massively and I have been given wrong information regarding policy excess on more than one occasion which led me to raise a complaint - months later they eventually agreed to pay but I am still waiting for the payment to be made.

Having been diagnosed with Bowel Cancer the ABC HealthCare, and in particular the Oncology team have been brilliant.. they have quick approved all necessary treatment and even sent me flowers when I came out of hospital

Having logged a claim on the online system, you can't even see there is a claim (pending or otherwise) until they've approved it, at which point you don't need to look at it. This meant I logged the same claim twice as it looked like the first claim had not been logged as there was no evidence of it on the site.

Hi Claire,  Regarding your response, do you  honestly feel it was acceptable?  ABC HealthCare have many many customers complaining about this form and faced with the same issues as myself and you simply thank me! How does this help? I am already in contact with my HR department who were shocked at the way we are being treated by ABC HealthCare, you will be hearing from them very shortly, and I will be doing everything in my power to ensure that our company policy will not be renewed next April.

Highly recommend especially in these troubling times for the NHS, sadly we see the likes of ABC HealthCare as a top priority as is our health. So far it have proven invaluable to our needs, for quick, specialised medical advice & treatment. Highly recommended

Honestly, absolutely useless customer service. 3 weeks and claim still has not even been entered onto the system!!  So I claimed for a dental treatment. After 10 days nothing back from ABC HealthCare. Contacted them and they said IT had blocked the email. Okay, this can happen.  I called and spoke to someone who said they would unblock the email and get it dealt with. I was told they would be in touch quickly to finish the claim.  8 days later nothing.  Called again, spoke with someone and got transferred. Then transferred again and then back to the switch board.   And now having been on the phone an hour and 5 different people they still have nothing resolved. No one has taken any responsibility.   Terrible customer services department. Really budget.
Hopeless customer service  Can’t deal with complaints  Don’t uphold their commitment to you

Horrible horrible experience every time I needed anything. Firt time I had a referral from GP for a consultation yet was refused an authorisation code twice, only when I spoke to someone for the 3rd time did I get one - hours wasted. Second time I had a referral to see an ophthalmologist, but I was advised to self fund ot go via NHS - which made me laugh and cry at the same time to be honest as they did not mean it as a joke! I spoke to the eye clinic I chose, which is close to my home and the doctor is on the approved list from ABC HealthCare so you would think they would be OK with that - the clinic in fact advised that they get patients from ABC HealthCare funded by ABC HealthCare for consultations and treatments alike, yet I was refused the auth code 3 times. When I told the person on the phone how did their other customers get the authorisation code and why I can't get one for an ophthalmologist on their list, I was told it was the "policy" so I asked if I needed to have a different policy since other people were able to get in and I can't and what the name of the policy is that I need then - they said no that would not help me and that they can't help me, but they are "sorry". I don't know why I was discriminated against seeing the clinic I wanted to go to recieved patients with same issues - no answer provided by ABC HealthCare. I finally asked for a complaint to be raised, but honestly, when you have a health condition that needs treating, you don't really have time to wait for 8 weeks to see what the decision is on your case. Extremely disappointing, sad experience. I would never ever recommend this company if their advise is to self fund or go via NHS, why am I paying for the policy then? I fear to imagine how people with more serious conditions must feel when they talk to this insensitive company and they are refused despite being a paying customer for years - when you are going through health issues last thing you need is this. Btw my consultantion and possible treatment was neither very expensive nor something "off label"... Needless to say I am not going to wait to be in a more serious situation again to see what would happen then - was brought to tears enough already. Please please avoid at all cost.

Titles: Hideous, deliberate refusal to understand or help
Review: I am absolutely livid at the sheer incompetence and refusal to behave like human beings and actually use common sense.   I made a clear typographical error on my pre treatment form.  I then got this re-verified by my GP.   HUMANS MAKE MISTAKES.  This is not something I deliberately did.  However, ABC HealthCare is deliberately now trying to scam me and say this is a pre-existing condition.  This is CLEARLY a typographical error or the previous insurer, AXA wouldn't have covered me.   My employer without my consent transferred me from AXA to ABC HealthCare.  I made a mistake on a form.  My GP verified the details.   This is all absolutely ludicrous and has completely interrupted my treatment of what is a very serious health condition.   I am getting zero resolution and it is just sheer and wilful incompetence and disrespect.

I am calling ABC HealthCare on 0345 608 0898 and having been waiting for 50 mins. I presumed my local GP was hardest to call, today I found ABC HealthCare was more difficult. I am a new member for ABC HealthCare. Still waiting.

Titles: I am happy with what has been covered, but…
Review: I am happy with what has been covered so far for my policies, but the bureaucracy could honestly not be any worse (and that is the feedback I get from any practitioner). Emails get answered once, then not again. Often initial replies are not dealing with the actual queries and/or are incomplete and/or vague. So are many replies via text that only tell me a decision has been made, but not what in regards to at all, and I then have to be in a queue for at least an hour again to find out, as I otherwise cannot get in touch. Also, the premium (=monthly free) increased by almost 50%, which is just robbery and unfair.

Titles: I am having a terrible time with them
Review: I am having a terrible time with them.  Waiting months and months for them to pay back a hospital invoice I paid and they still have not repaid us......they are insanely bureaucratic.  I picked them as they are a charity but really I now wish I had chosen a private health insurer.  They are not paying us because they want the hospital to re invoice them so they pay and then the hospital pay us.  BUT they are arguing with the hospital because they want to be invoiced at a lower rate as ABC HealthCare get a discount when it is a client. Nothing is happening and it has been months.  You cannot trust what the Customer Support team  and even the Complaints Team say because they lie so that you close your complaint.  This is to manipulate the Customer complaints data they publish. I have escalated my complaint to Financial Ombudsman to investigate both my payment and the way they are manipulating complaint figures.  USE A DIFFERENT COMPANY FOR YOUR MEDICAL INSURANCE. READ THE OTHER BAD REVIEWS ITS NOT JUST ME COMPLAINING ABOUT THIS.

Titles: I am having counselling through ABC HealthCare…
Review: I am having counselling through ABC HealthCare after going through a terrible time at the start of the year. ABC HealthCare made the process painless and straight forward, as they were really helpful and easy to deal with.

I am no spring chicken and pay a hefty amount for my ABC HealthCare cover. I am also the director of the company which currently has a group policy (which I am now actively looking to move elsewhere). I was warned not to go with ABC HealthCare when I took out the policy because of this very thing but assumed it wouldn't really be true as they are such a well known company. I was wrong.  The issue: I have arthritis from old age in my big toe and it no longer bends very well. I got an x-ray and during the x-ray I said to the nurse as a throwaway comment that it was stood on 10 years ago and that may have been the start of the issue, the x-ray report said it was old age but the issue started 10 years ago. ABC HealthCare assessed the report and said that because I reported it was stood on 10 years ago it was a 'pre-existing condition'! Though I never seen a doctor or anyone about it previously. I have also had medical cover with AXA for the previous ~7 years and there was no need to get it sorted then as it was not an issue. I've had aches and pains all over at one point or another; does this mean the older you get the more the cover costs but they will cover less of your body? Feels like they want the best of both worlds.  When the chap called to tell me I was not covered he said I need to get another letter from my doctor to say that the incident 10 years ago and the arthritis are not related and they can reconsider. I am not sure how that helps and find it ridiculous that they request this letter, I'm also not sure my doctor could make that call given their info as I have never spoken to them in the past about my toe!  There was no appeal process explained and I was left frustrated at their decision with no way to challenge it.  It appears they now want £100 for their GP services from me, but this is (TBC) because the letter is very confusing.  If ABC HealthCare use this sort of excuse not to honour their cover then not only is it not suitable for me, but it is not suitable for my staff who may need to reply on it.  I recommend using AXA or one of the others over ABC HealthCare based on my experience.  Edit 1: Fair play to ABC HealthCare for getting back to me last night.  The bill for £100 was not discussed and I am still unsure if they expect me to pay £100 more for them not to cover me.  The scenarios below assume you have been with ABC HealthCare for a 12 healthy months.  I had a chat with their customer service who explained that because I told the doctor someone stood on my foot 10 years ago and that may have been the start of it, that meant it was a pre-existing condition and that I should  have reported it to them (note: I have never seen anyone medical about it in the past as it was not doctor worthy, even though I had medical cover previously with AXA for many years) . I clearly stated it was not bothering me and has not been a cause for concern for 10 years or I would have got it looked at!  I then asked 'hypothetically', if I had a snapped Achilles 10 years ago but it was not bothering me when I took out the cover, should I have mentioned that she said 'no, not if it's not bothering you'. I asked how is this different. If I had and issue with my Achilles and it was sore, I would mention to the doctor that I snapped it 10 years ago, surely they would use the same logic.   Ignoring all that: As you get older you may start to suffer from arthritis or notice a change that doesn’t immediately bother you, I'm not a doctor, but assume it's  something which you don't worry about immediately but as time goes on you decide enough is enough and go to the doctor. If you are to be honest with the doctor and say it's been like this for 1.5 years, ABC HealthCare WILL NOT COVER YOU.   People be warned, this cover is IMHO not suitable for elderly people. What about in the case of something that comes on slowly, a slow cancer? If you tell the doctor your bowels have been loose for years (maybe even if they haven't but you want to make it more important) ABC HealthCare WILL NOT COVER YOU.  The customer service lady did explain that they'll raise an appeal and request more info from my doctor.  Ignoring my trivial scenario (it’s a big toe I can’t move, and I’m sure they NHS will sort it), I want health cover where you can tell the truth about when a symptom may have started. I expect in the case of cancer that could be very important!  I appreciate ABC HealthCare can't go into my details here which is why I have created the scenarios above, where it appears ABC HealthCare would not cover you for serious illness, if you have a policy with them and something that previously was an irritation turns into something important. In these cases, it appears you have to tell the doctor you got ill on that day to get cover.   I understand why they have this rule, it is to stop someone taking out a policy, getting something fixed and then quitting ABC HealthCare. In my case this is my company policy. I choose ABC HealthCare and cancelled my AXA policy, which would have covered me. I'm clearly not looking to game the system.
I am really happy with the speed in which I was able to speak to an expert and explain my issue.  I was referred to a specialist the same day.

I am unable to register on line. I have emailed sand called. I missed 2 calls from you and tried to call back many times, but after being on hold for over 42 mins I gave up. I wrote to you saying I wanted to cancel as I was having dental treatment. I have now gone with Denplan. I am a corporate member so am guessing I cant cancel so I will just have to carry on paying two insurances this year till I can cancel. Would not recommend to any one.
