Titles: Disappointing Experience with ABC HealthCare Customer Service
Review: I am writing to express my deep disappointment with the recent experiences I have had with ABC HealthCare's customer service. Over the past two months, I have encountered multiple issues that have significantly affected my trust and satisfaction with your services.  In April, I visited a ABC HealthCare clinic for a consultation following a bike accident. The doctor advised me to undergo a CT scan on the same day. However, shortly after the consultation, I received a text message from ABC HealthCare requesting a call back. Upon calling, I was informed by the customer service representative that they were unaware of the reason for the text message. It was during this conversation that I discovered the CT scan was not covered within the initial consultation. Regrettably, this information was not communicated to me adequately, leading to confusion and frustration. Although the customer service representative assured me that there would be no additional cost, I later received an invoice for the CT scan, contradicting the assurance provided.  More recently, I have been experiencing severe pain in my feet, which has been significantly impacting my daily activities, both personally and professionally. I took the initiative to locate a clinic near my workplace to seek a consultation. I received authorization from the individual I spoke with over the phone at ABC HealthCare, believing that I would be covered for the necessary treatments. Following the consultation and an X-ray, I was informed by the attending physician that surgery was required. To my dismay, when I contacted ABC HealthCare and provided the surgery authorization code, I was informed that the doctor who evaluated me was not covered by my membership. This revelation came as a complete surprise, as no prior communication indicated any restrictions regarding the choice of physicians within my coverage. This unforeseen circumstance has caused an extensive delay in my surgery, exacerbating my pain and further disrupting my daily life.  These instances of inadequate information, miscommunication, and conflicting advice have resulted in significant frustration and inconvenience for me. As a loyal ABC HealthCare member, I had always relied on the company's professionalism, expertise, and dedication to customer care. However, these recent experiences have shaken my confidence in the quality of service provided by ABC HealthCare.  I kindly request that you thoroughly investigate these matters and take appropriate action to rectify the issues I have encountered. I believe it is essential for ABC HealthCare to improve the knowledge and training of your customer service representatives to ensure accurate and consistent information is provided to members.

Titles: Bad experience with the first claim, no help from ABC HealthCare
Review: I booked an appointment with a doctor through their website. It's the first time I made a claim and specifically picked a clinician who was "fee-assured". Despite that, 2 appointments and some blood tests run over £1700 - almost double my policy limit. At no point during that I was informed what costs will be (only the initial appointment and follow up, which were only about £200). Some tests, upon further inspection, didn't seem related to my condition and some were recently done by the NHS. Moreover, the doctor charged me for an appointment to which he was over 30 minutes late - I couldn't wait that long and let the reception know. Despite all that, when I complained with ABC HealthCare, they were not interested in solving this issue and only advised to contact the hospital directly. I understand hospital is at fault here, but I pay for the insurance and hospital was supposed to be "fee-assured", so doesn't the insurer have some responsibility in helping with this?

Titles: Hassle free and very good assistance
Review: I called this morning to apply for a policy for me and my wife and it was very easy to set it up, specially thanks to Rose which was very knowledgeable, kind and very supportive. Thumbs up for ABC HealthCare and Rose :)

Titles: I called up ABC HealthCare got appointment in a…
Review: I called up ABC HealthCare got appointment in a few days had a xray same day had results same day Fantastic no waiting. Lovely staff and very helpful. Thank you

Titles: NHS service is better than this
Review: I cannot begin to tell you about how bad the service I have received. Over 6 months ago ABC HealthCare gave me information which delayed my claim, this led to me paying for treatment which took 5 months and a mix up to get the money back. Further treatment - I was given a new code for a scan. Then two months later the computer has rejected the claim so the hospital are billing me direct for the money. I have surgery in 2 weeks and I am scared they refuse this payment too. Passed from person to person. No one rings back, promised a call back today and nothing, I have never experienced anything like this. Even my consultant said ABC HealthCare are awful. I hope someone calls me on the back after this review. I think the NHS would be better than the service I have received.

Titles: ABC HealthCare says filling but no cavity in sight!
Review: I commend ABC HealthCare on its prompt payment of dental claims, but this is far outweighed by its (knowingly?) poor categorisation of some treatments such as compound fillings. I fully expect to have to refer the case to the ombudsman after 8 weeks. ABC HealthCare insists on names matching documents but failed to send out any policy documents after two months, and continues to use "other" as the prefix to my name. All in all not a great experience.

I contacted ABC HealthCare regarding getting some advice on my neck and shoulder pain. They were easy to contact and suggest a telephone conversation with a physiotherapist, which they arranged for me straight away at a convenient time for me. After my telephone conversation with the physiotherapist he suggested that I needed to see a physio for help. He emailed details of several physio clinics nearby, I contacted one of them and made an appointment. I found the whole process simple, straight forward and easy. Everyone involved explained everything thoroughly and answered any questions I had. I was very happy with the whole process.

Titles: I contacted ABC HealthCare to get an appointment…
Review: I contacted ABC HealthCare to get an appointment with a dermatologist. They provided me with 3 names of Doctors, and none of them are still working with ABC HealthCare. After contacting all the 3 professionals and hearing that they are not with ABC HealthCare anymore, I had to ask for other professionals' indications. I only found one with availability 45 days after my contact.  It was awful for private and expensive health insurance that should have updated records.

Titles: I could not fault the level of…
Review: I could not fault the level of attention to the delivery of my treatment. I am very happy.

I could not get through on the phone line, however the process online was simpler and I got my authorisation through.  I was pleased this included the consultation and possible follow-up X-ray and MRI scan etc without having to seek approval for each, this is much better than the last time I used the service.

Titles: I don't like some of the bureaucracy…
Review: I don't like some of the bureaucracy like having to call always to log a claim with no alternative digital approach, or having to pay for the GP to confirm something is not a pre-existing condition. Other than that though I haven't had a problem with them.

Titles: I don’t see how they add value vs NHS
Review: I don’t see how they add value vs NHS. They don’t inform you on what will require further payments. Much better ABC HealthCare in other countries.

I found the whole process very smooth indeed. I have spoken with quite a few people on the phone at ABC HealthCare and all have been friendly and very professional and knowledge. I would definitely recommend based on my experience.

Titles: I got a fast response to my concern.
Review: I got a fast response to my concern.
I got a text saying I have been credited about a third of my claim, very belatedly, but, with two claims outstanding, no clue as to which one was being paid.

Titles: I got immediate response with choices…
Review: I got immediate response with choices offered to me of both consultants and hospitals

Titles: I got hit by a double whammy on the excess
Review: I had 3 treatments (each cost £75), each a week apart and I was told that any excesses would apply in a text. The annual excess, it turns out is £100. What I didn't know was that the year end for excess would rollover between the first and second treatment. So I got nothing for the first, or second and £50 for the third. So out of a total cost of £225 I received £50 worth of cover. Had anyone told me the year was about to rollover (in the middle of may) I could have benefitted from another £100.
I had a call with a specialist cardiac nurse after my CABG surgery. I found him very reassuring and his advice very helpful.

Titles: I had a great experience with Adam a real professional who takes his work very seriously.
Review: I had a great experience with Adam extending my ABC HealthCare membership. He was very helpful and knowledgeable answering all our questions over the phone and promptly replying to our emails with the right answers every time. A real professional who takes his work very seriously.  I've been a ABC HealthCare member for the last 8 years and never had to complain about the service provided to me and my family members.
I had a great online appointment recently. All my concerns were listened to and addressed. I felt relaxed and unhurried. My doctor was friendly, supportive and instructive. Im very happy.

I had a knee operation following a private health assessment where I was referred to a knee specialist. I also benefited from follow on Physio treatment afterwards to build back the strength in my knee. Claiming etc. was easy and I had no issues with any of that side of the process. The whole thing was made very easy by ABC HealthCare.

Titles: I had a lovely experience visiting…
Review: I had a lovely experience visiting ABC HealthCare- all the staff were warm and welcoming.
I had a phone call, but the person was unable to do anything until my GP completed a form. Form delivered to GP. Nothing else. I am in pain and wonder where to get some help?

Titles: I had a really negative experience with…
Review: I had a really negative experience with ABC HealthCare recently, with no medical assistance provided for an extremely painful left arm.   Just questions about eligibility to claim through my company provided scheme along with expected to complete a large questionnaire partly by myself and completed by my GP, which is not easy to get appointments with. The forms then have to be signed electronically but the form does not allow it to be signed.   I will probably ask the company to cancel my membership to ABC HealthCare and at least save the tax on this company benefit.

Titles: I had anthroplasty, ABC HealthCare won't approve therapy!
Review: I had anthroplasty.  Need therapy.  Got 5 sessions.  Therapist then asked for 10.  ABC HealthCare said only 5 more and THAT IS FINAL.  Ask any single Doctor if 10 therapy sessions are enough after anthroplasty.  They'll laugh.  It is so so so obvious to anyone that you need more.  Oops, sorry.  Obvious to anyone but ABC HealthCare.  Ludicrous.

Titles: I had concerns after a colapse at work
Review: I had concerns after a colapse at work. Though all of my symptoms were pointing to a faint, at 60 years old, you could imagine that my mind needed to be very much assured that no pending ailments were present and lurking in the background. From the minute i called, the service was provided in a very professional manner. Well done to all of the staff involved and well done for getting myself fit and back to work.

I had hysterectomy surgery carried out at Lincoln Hospital, consultants name was Mr Otiri. The overall experience and high level of care provided was incredible. I feel forever grateful and couldn’t recommend Lincoln Hospital enough. Thank you

Titles: I had major back surgery and generally…
Review: I had major back surgery and generally things went smoothly with ABC HealthCare. When going through major surgery that is out of the everyday it would be good to have a named contact. Often reps on the telephone didn’t seem to understand the complexities of my situation. They were always helpful however and confirmed things quickly.
I had my enquiry dealt with quickly and efficiently by Yahya on the phone. This resulted in me getting an appointment with a consultant within the week. Excellent service.
I had my treatment authorised in a matter of hours and had it all sorted very quickly

I had no idea that I had an excess of £500 . I am a new customer and very dissatisfied , I was not told when I made my appointments for my cancer testing , and of course had to pay after HCA etc chased me for the money .
I had to speak to two different people at ABC HealthCare for my recent experience and and would have preferred to deal with the same person who knew my details.

Titles: I had to spend  a few days in NHS…
Review: I had to spend  a few days in NHS hospital ABC HealthCare sent me compensation without any drama well pleased.

I had to try many times to get my telephone call answered by the call centre. After four times where I waited for an hour, I rang off after an hours wait, I at last got through after a 30 minute wait. Even that I feel is not acceptable. They need more call handlers as a wait of over 30 minutes is unacceptable. This was not a single occurrence as I have had cause to phone several times since and had a similar experiences

Titles: I had to wait over 60 mins twice on…
Review: I had to wait over 60 mins twice on Mental Health line and in the end had to email but took took an additional email to get a clear answer on getting authorisation for treatment

Titles: I hate the dentist and the wait times…
Review: I hate the dentist and the wait times but this process and the dentist was amazing.

Titles: I have ABC HealthCare dental care and the ease of…
Review: I have ABC HealthCare dental care and the ease of submitting and getting payments is incredible. Two fillings this morning and payment approved before anesethtic wore off!   do the maths on premium v what it pays out in each category to know whether it's worth it but as a product it's simple to use

Titles: I have PMI with ABC HealthCare and I am totally…
Review: I have PMI with ABC HealthCare and I am totally satisfied with all the processes to get authorisation number, to book a consultant and all else after that and in between. Whatever you need you can find info in the website and it can be followed easily.

I have Stage 4 breast cancer and ABC HealthCare have covered everything that my Oncologist has asked for including telephone counselling and drugs that are not yet available in Wales. I cannot speak highly enough of them and the Oncology team is second to none. Without ABC HealthCare I may not be here now.I have had seven years of care and support and advocate to all who can afford it to join ABC HealthCare. You never know what’s around the corner. I was 37 on initial diagnosis.

Titles: Nice customer service by ABC HealthCare
Review: I have always experience on time support and correct answer from ABC HealthCare with regards to my medical insurance

Titles: I have always found the Customer…
Review: I have always found the Customer Service team to be kind, and courteous online and on the telephone..   On the phone is the better and quickest way to make contact with them, as it's so much quicker to deal with your enquiry that way..   Doing it online seems to take a lot longer as they're dealing with more than one customer at a time.. This then gets frustrating when you have to repeat yourself and go through your enquiry a few times before getting your enquiry completed especially when you're after an authorisation code.   Apart from that everytime I have dealt with them I have not had any issues with them.

I have an excess on my policy to keep the costs down and ABC HealthCare have been excellent in forwarding the claim statements to keep me informed. I get good advice when phoning for authorisation codes and unlike many customer service departments feel that these folk are on my side side.

I have been a customer of ABC HealthCare for a long while and they are always happy to help. They communicate well, the are polite and explain everything to you. They are professional but also caring. ABC HealthCare go above and beyond for there customers and I’ve never been refused cover for treatment.

Titles: I have been on ABC HealthCare for 2-3 years
Review: I have been on ABC HealthCare for 2-3 years, either privately or through work. Whenever I change from work to private I select to continue based on the work history.  Every time it changes from work to private, ABC HealthCare enforces a 3 month moratorium for any exam/scan/diagnosis. This is unacceptable as they already have the GP history and full history.  I do NOT recommend ABC HealthCare.  Additionally, my wife wanted to do some ear cleaning, something that could be done in a pharmacy. ABC HealthCare wanted to put that on her file as a hear loss! it took them only 3 weeks to reply to let us know that they could not cover it, especially within the 3 month period.   Tired and fed up with them.
I have been on hold for 1:30hr mid day on a Friday. Prat that your registration works or good luck! Ridiculous waiting time.

I have been on their dental insurance and submitting claims have been easy and the money has arrived fairly promptly.  I recently signed up for their health insurance through work and it has been impossible to sign up  I have tried calling 4 times over the last 2 days. The last time I got through to a customer support rep and he hung up on me.

Titles: I have been until now well look
Review: I have been until now well look

Titles: Awful Company That Used to be Good
Review: I have been with ABC HealthCare for over 25 years and I haven't used them for the past 10 years.  My experience of getting an ENT consultation and tests done made the whole situation very stressful.  Having complained and they said they would cover certain invoices I am getting chasing letters from teh consultants office, the hospital and ABC HealthCare.  They have not upheld what they confirmed to me and causing me a lot of wasted time in sorting out their extremely poor service.  Think twice about using them!

Titles: I have been with ABC HealthCare for 50 years but…
Review: I have been with ABC HealthCare for 50 years but I'm finding it near impossible to get through. Sometimes I have to wait ages for someone to answer. And the music is awful.

Titles: UNBELIEVABLE WAIT TIMES TO GET THROUGH!!!…
Review: I have been with ABC HealthCare for 6years- all was well until this year - I am always having trouble even getting through to an advisor - waiting up to 53 mins onetime, I had to give up and start again, this took another 30mins  we are told to call and get authorisation , I was in a hospital receiption on 19.4.23 after seeing my surgeon, we wanted authority to have me taken in as an emergency case that night for surgery and intravenous medication, I was held on hold, and in pain for 35 mins !!! to ABC HealthCare to get clearance - THIS IS NOT THE FIRST TIME, I ALSO TRIED ONLINE AND DIFFERENT NUMBERS, I REALISE NHS IS STRUGGLING BUT WHY IS MY PAID FOR EXPERIENCE BAD NOW TOO.I have sent off a complaint to customer services with a few screenshots of my wait times , but still not heard back !!!!

Titles: I have been with ABC HealthCare for a few years…
Review: I have been with ABC HealthCare for a few years now and they are very helpful and quick with getting your problem fixed.

Titles: I have found people I have dealt with…
Review: I have found people I have dealt with to be helpful and understanding

I have had a fantastic experience with ABC HealthCare. My calls have always been quickly answered and professionally dealt with. This is an amazing service. The online claim i made was completed within a couple of hours of submission.

Titles: I have had a few health issues over the…
Review: I have had a few health issues over the last few months and ABC HealthCare have always been fantastic supporting me with these

Titles: Impossible to get help or use the service
Review: I have had a membership for 2 years and tried to contact them yesterday for the first time. I already have a referral from my GP. ABC HealthCare's phones said they were busy, go online. 40mins and 3 operators later someone finally answered online. They barely spoke English and repeated themselves, getting nowhere fast. Finally they said I had to fill in a form and they would get me a choice of consultants. This took a long time. But the form is for new members or to add a dependant. A form I completed when I joined. This is irrelevant and lengthy, clearly wrong. I don't want a new membership and I don't want to add a dependant, so now I have no choice but to either try contacting them again...which was beyond painful, or just go back to waiting another 7 months on the NHS. At least that's free. Next step, cancel my policy. Useless. I'm in agony and ABC HealthCare don't care.  I tried calling again on Saturday 3rd June (above was Thursday 1st June), spoke to a helpful guy who informed me there was a marker on my account by mistake which said forms had not been received, but they had, so it would be removed which would then allow me to get treatment. He would then call me on Monday (5th) after work to update me and see about the next step. Today is Monday. No call. How surprising. So do I make a 4th attempt to get treatment? What am I paying for??  2nd update. I contacted ABC HealthCare for a 4th time on Tuesday (6th). They had more questions about my history that they never bothered chasing when I joined, so are now causing delays. I was told that now I had answered them, this should now resolve the matter and they will call me on Thursday (8th June) to update me and hopefully give me the consultation choices. Guess what? It's Friday 9th June and I haven't had a phonecall back. Officially a complete rip off. I have been paying for a service I was never going to have access to because they never contacted me to complete anything they wanted answers to. I was totally unaware of any issues. Now they keep promising a call back and never deliver. Every call I make is met with the same response...call you back. The NHS costs less and I will likely be seen sooner than ABC HealthCare will call me. Don't pay for it. Truly not worth the money or even the paper anything is written on by them. I am left in real pain and nobody at ABC HealthCare cares. They just want your money, like any insurance company, they will find any excuse not to pay out. STAY AWAY.

Titles: Great service whilst dealing with multiple conditions
Review: I have had the misfortune of needing to have a number of different potentially very serious conditions all being examined within the space of a few weeks. The speed of approving each of my treatments was fantastic and the quality of consultation, procedures and care excellent.

I have had to use ABC HealthCare for 2 medical issues in the past few months and the ease of the website and helpfulness and quick response of customer service have been great.  Both medical issues were resolved quickly thanks to the help of ABC HealthCare.

Titles: I have never been so disappointed
Review: I have never been so disappointed.  Signed up via the business I work for, tried to claim for the first time in years and suddenly every single obstacle was thrown up.  I ended up with 3 different membership numbers (why I have no idea), staff on the phones were very inexperienced and unable to help, claim was turned down even after I provided everything they could possibly have needed.  I have literally spent hours and hours on the phones over the last 3 weeks trying to make sense of what the problem is. I wouldn't mind if I didn't follow their booklet and get pre-authorisation - I did! The company I work for is massive and today I spoke to HR about the issues and they said they are looking into swapping to another medical insurance company because they've had so many issues.

Titles: Sad that a once great company is so bad at customer relations....
Review: I have never called them in the last 20 years without receiving the message 'our lines are extremely busy, you may want to try later': longest wait 50 minutes.   Before 2000, it was easy to get through.   Their customer relations, trying to make amends, said last Thursday that they were attaching a direct line for me to ring - you've guessed it, no number attached.   Despite sending an email that day no response had been received by the following Wednesday - yesterday - and I had no code for treatment booked for last evening.   They are however 5* at taking your money.  Can anyone recommend  a company that treats us  better?
I have quite a few problems at the moment and using ABC HealthCare to find the right consultants. They `re always super helpful and quick to authorize treatments.  Thank you

Titles: Dispute of claim despite all relevant paperwork supplied.
Review: I have the highest level cover and have supplied all of the paper required, but ABC HealthCare are disputing an orthordontistry claim. I'm not happy and the response time is terrible.

I have used AXA in the past and due to changing employers now have ABC HealthCare. I was sent lists of doctors that were either too far or no appointment for weeks. I managed to book an appointment with a doctor who shockingly told me to go to my NHS GP for bloods even though ABC HealthCare has authorised bloods. I'm appalled by the whole process. What a waste of time I could have just gone to my NHS doctor in the first place. I'm furious doesn't even come close!

I have used ABC HealthCare on several occasions over the last three years and in each case my experience has been of a professional caring organisation. The consultants are knowledgeable and explain what they are going to do. They also listen to your concerns and answer questions in a way you can understand. I have used them for both minor and major health concerns including major operations. Knowledgeable, no fuss, actionable, honest and trustworthy.

Titles: I joined ABC HealthCare to avoid delays but they're built in anyway
Review: I joined ABC HealthCare to avoid delays in the NHS. Unfortunately in the first year of membership, for every new claim ABC HealthCare require a form signed by your GP - which in my case took over 3 weeks to get. Really frustrating.

I just don't understand. I haven't seen the doctor in over 5 years about the issue. But the NHS doctor puts treatment ongoing on the form and I'm denied help from ABC HealthCare. I dispute it and they suddenly say I saw them 4.5 years ago. Then I'm told I can get cover to see a specialist who will make a report. Then the specialists I contact have no idea what I'm talking about and that that's not how this works. I've waited months now after paying ABC HealthCare for years. If its going to be this much hassle every time i try and get help from ABC HealthCare I just don't wanna be with them.  I wanted to get away from the NHS but you still have to go through useless NHS GPs to get help. When I got the form from the GP (£15 and two weeks later) I was happy. It said the last time i saw them about it was way over 5 years ago. That should be the end of it, it's not pre existing. But they wrote treatment ongoing, which it isn't. Then I have to wait weeks for the dispute to be dealt with and I'm told I saw them 4.5 years ago which wasn't what they put on the form! Plus I've been told that the underwriting staff can decide its preexisting even if it's over 5 years....is there a limit? Can they refuse something as preexisting if its been ten years?! What is the point. Its so hard to get help from them you're better off just staying home and fixing medical issues yourself. I was telling everyone to go private. Not any more

I just joined ABC HealthCare (01/05/23) & was unfortunate to spend 7 nights in my local hospital. I claimed for ABC HealthCares overnight stay cash benefit & ABC HealthCare paid the benefit to me within a week. I only contacted ABC HealthCare through their chat line & greceived the details on how to claim from there.

I like the service provided by the dentist.   I dislike that I pay a benefit in kind for this but it still doesn’t cover the Routine checkups.   Have to pay out of my own pocket plus tax to get routine checks.

Titles: Not clear on process for using Babylon
Review: I like the speed at which I was able to make an appointment online. However, I was subsequently told my ID was not verified, which meant there would be a delay. I ended up going through my own GP in the end. So it all seemed a bit pointless. I still dont know if my ID has been verified or not, so not sure if its worth keeping this insurance on?

Titles: A medical insurance company more like a mental health cause!
Review: I made a claim for nhs cash benefit after being admitted to hospital with a potential bleed on the brain.  I have been so misled and misinformed on so many occasions call backs never happened incorrect information given fobbed off. I made it clear I couldn’t accept a cheque due to having an online bank the advisor told me bacs would be possible and it would be paid that way for then a cheque to be posted to me that I can’t cash. Being off work sick on ssp and now having to call every day chasing the bacs payment has caused me so much stress and delayed my recovering time. I can honestly say it has made me mentally suffer. So may staff unable to help and giving incorrect information and then not actioning tasks that could of made this process so much easier and stress free it’s so uncalled for and so unfair when you pay for a medical service that has honestly made my mental health suffer. Absolutely terrible

Titles: I must admit I am so impressed with…
Review: I must admit I am so impressed with ABC HealthCare. I have dental insurance through my work and submitting my claim online was an easy process and within 2 working days I had received a text to say my claim had been processed and 3 days later, it was in my bank account. Very appreciative for how quick the process was especially with the cost of private dental treatment!

Titles: For urgent matters please give a better service!
Review: I need a scan to rule out cancer as the reason for my pain.  I had two referrals (GP and Babylon).  I submitted the GP one on the ABC HealthCare portal and waited 3 days.  I heard nothing.  Eventually I called, waited to speak to someone.  I got given a authorisation code but I asked what I was authorised for.  I got transferred and was told I needed to see a consultant to have a scan done so my referral wasnt valid.  I was then asked questions and told I could have access to a consultant.  I stated my two local private hospitals and was told there was only one consultant that worked out of both I could try and get an appointment with.  I quickly messaged my friend that used to work at one of the hospitals who gave me two other names.  The ABC HealthCare rep then typed in their names and said they were there and no idea why they didnt come up on the search.  Awful service when you are worried and just want to get to see someone asap.  My friend also said that I didnt need to see a consultant to have a scan done, they were done at the hospital all the time with just a GP referral through private health cover.
I needed some support quickly prior to a hospital appointment and was very grateful to the person dealing with my call for his empathy and help to provide what I needed efficiently.

I pay £60 per month for ABC HealthCare.  I tried to make a claim because of a shoulder injury. I've spoken to the GP / Physio over the phone who promised to refer me further within 2 hours.  However, both ABC HealthCare and the physio therapist didn't have my email on file.... So I've never heard from them for 2 months even though I chased regularly.  I decided to pay for my own ultra sound scan and get physio treatments etc. In those 2 months ABC HealthCare haven't tried to rectify the problem and offer me another consultation or treatment but I was still paying £60 per month.  Their customer service is awful, never received any refund or compensation even though I didn't receive any treatment and my data was misplaced due to them not having my email address on file.  Please avoid ABC HealthCare. Individual customers are treated like a numbers game, they only care about large corporate contracts  They had plenty of opportunities to rectify a simple problem which resulted in me paying each month but not receiving any treatment - total JOKE for such a reputable brand  @ Claire: Your written response offer was a £50 payment even though I've spent C£400 on the membership to date and over £500 of my own money on scans and treatments. Not to mention that you stored my data for 2 months with no purpose which is a Gdpr breach (physio spoken to me on the phone but didn't send me or you the report because my email address wasn't on the system... ) You haven't provided me with any solution or an adequate resolution....

I phoned ABC HealthCare on Tuesday 23rd May, was given a telephone appointment for Wednesday 24th May and then referred to a consultant who saw me on Thursday 25th May. Extremely fast & Professional Service from start to finish.

I purchased the policy and ensured that existing investigations would be covered as they had been with a company policy. After confirming this several times at point of purchase I went ahead and placed the cover. However at point of claim it took 2 weeks, 4 phone calls and several webchats to get it agreed.itbwasnonoy the day before my appointment that I made contact and obtained my authorisation number. No contact had been made with me despite several chasers.

Titles: I received excellent treatment from Mr…Michael Stellakis
Review: I received excellent treatment from Mr Michael Stellakis and all the nursing staff and operating theatre team at The Nuffield Hospital, Leamington Spa.I felt so well looked after the whole time. I believe I experienced the best possible and available care from an outstanding surgeon and his nursing staff and team.

Titles: Health review not for the individual. Especially if pregnant.
Review: I recently attended my 'Be Ahead' health review with ABC HealthCare in Reading. The final report doesn't even mention the fact I am 4 months pregnant. This service is entirely a conveyor belt and doesn't provide an  individualized view of your health at all. Very disappointed. In addition the online complaints form doesn't work.

Titles: I run a business but find ABC HealthCare…
Review: I run a business but find ABC HealthCare impossible to contact. Their chatlines are often closed, emails unanswered or ignored and phones never picked up. Their new TOUCH and Blua Apps are impossible to register with their Digital Team seem to be lost in issues !

I saw a specialist for a bowel problem . I had first class attention from the whole team. I had a CT scan , bloods taken and a colonoscopy in just over two weeks. Nothing was overlooked to give me peace of mind. I got the reassurance I needed.

I spoke to ABC HealthCare healthyminds. I have spoken to them 3 times in total and have been lucky enough to have the same lady each time. Olivia. She is absolutely amazing and an absolute asset to your team. I am forever grateful  for her help..

Titles: Unacceptably long wait times for calls and authorisation of visits risation
Review: I submitted a preauthorisation request and on 25 April 2023 was sent a text to say that this has been received and I will be contacted once it had been assessed. I tried calling the helpline several times but aborted mission after 15 minutes of waiting. Eventually I did get through on 19th June and the preauthorisation permission hadn't been processed yet but was processed on that day. Two months is an unacceptable time to wait for a response and the waiting times on the helpline are also unreasonable

I totally agree with the reviewers that say that they had to wait an unreasonable time to get through on the phone. I had an awful experience trying to justify any treatment I asked for. I would not recommend ABC HealthCare to anyone! I ended up getting the help I needed from the NHS eventually anyway. I lost a lot of money and got nothing in return!
I tried , three different days to get through on the phone to get a referral number . Then the waiting time was so long hanging on . My phone bill , was on my mind as I listen to music .

Titles: I used ABC HealthCare Insurance to diagnose and…
Review: I used ABC HealthCare Insurance to diagnose and address the issues with my back in 2022. The service was not short of brilliant; the diagnostic included an MRI scan and assessment by a physiotherapist and a specialist doctor. I was given access to the app with exercises selected for me with plenty of advice on how to proceed. Last year I added dental cover that I had claimed already as I needed to have two feelings replaced. Dealing with ABC HealthCare is pleasant and quick. Peace of mind is priceless!
I used the web chat which was great and was being physio triaged within an hour or so. Experienced physio who was lovely and we have a plan!
I waited a minimum of an hour on the phone to speak with a representative to get a physio referral.

Titles: I waited only a couple of days for my…
Review: I waited only a couple of days for my initial consultation at the PMC, St John’s Wood that took place on the 25th of April.   Although the appointment went well, I was advised by the shoulder/ arm consultant to arrange an MRI and to book an appointment with a spinal specialist. The MRI took place on the 27th of April at the Spire Centre Elstree however, the recommended consultant was not available until the 18th of May. I managed to see an alternative on the 3rd of May, also at the Spire Centre, Elstree.   During this appointment I was advised to see a pain management specialist but sadly this consultant is not available until the 5th of June and the soonest possible date for treatment, the 19th of June.   Almost two months will have elapsed since my initial consultation and nearly three months since my symptoms began.   I will work to improve my wait time.   For the avoidance of doubt, everyone I’ve seen or interacted with has been incredibly professional, helpful and friendly.

Titles: I was able to access a top rated…
Review: I was able to access a top rated Cardiologist the next day, which was a Saturday, and admitted to the hospital of my choice in two days.    The Cardiologist has been in constant contact throughout my treatment, which so far has required 3 procedures.    I have always been treated with the greatest respect, and have plenty of time to ask any questions about my treatment. I have had private medical insurance since I was 28 years old.    Every now and again I have questioned, is it worth the premium, but it is so reassuring knowing I can have the best possible treatment immediately when I required it.

I was able to get really quick referral to a hand specialist and then to treatment using the chat part of the app. More importantly, we recently used ABC HealthCare insurance to get a diagnosis and rapid treatment for my husband who has cancer. Our concerns had been ignored by the NHS GP For the last year so we took the matter into our own hands and got a referral to a ABC HealthCare specialist. We were then pushed through the system very quickly with tests and diagnoses every couple of days and have had the most fantastic treatment from a multidisciplinary team, all through ABC HealthCare. He is now doing well and has finished four cycles of chemo in the same time it's taken others In the NHS fast-track to get the first treatment.  ABC HealthCare insurance has been a lifesaver and I would recommend it to anyone. Sadly, NHS coverage nowadays is simply not good enough.

I was doubtful about the effectiveness of a Phsyio triage by telephone but I was proved totally wrong.  My Physion, Anderson, was brilliant.  Easy to talk to, thorough, fully understood and perfectly translated my condition.  He offered immediate advice along with sending me contact details for the right physio services before the call was finished.  He even made me laugh which is not easy at the moment, with the pain I'm in.

I was informed when I got to reception after my visits that my insurance would not cover this visit. The receptionist said her computer was refusing to allow cover for this visit. It was only when she actually phoned the office for an explanation that consent was given. This has never been a problem before and I had to sit around, unnecessarily concerned that something was wrong with my cover. Eventually it was sorted, but not wholly happy.

Titles: Lengthy phone call without an answer
Review: I was kept on the phone for 26 minutes & my query did not get answered. There was a v noisy background. I was told I was being transferred to someone else 7 times

Titles: I was looking for  a great packages of…
Review: I was looking for  a great packages of dental insurance through on line and friends recommendation.Finally  ABC HealthCare is one of the best insurance we have the on line claim is very straightforward and easy they refunded on time. Customer service were all excellent. would highly recommended
I was made to feel really important and valued. Every stage of the appointment was explained to me clearly and I didn't have to wait long in-between tests.
I was not able to pass security by only giving my name after calling from my touch account. I was then out on hold for a long time

I was notified of my appointment well in advance and also just before. The actual appointment was punctual and I was greeted in a friendly professional manner. As a nervous patient, I was treated with understanding and  politeness, which made me feel at ease throughout. A great experience.

Titles: I wasn’t going to post something on…
Review: I wasn’t going to post something on Trustpilot, but I thought it was borderline comical that ABC HealthCare sent me a Trustpilot link requesting me to express how my recent experience was… I didn’t necessarily want to make this public, but since ABC HealthCare is still waiting to pay a claim which was submitted months ago with all the necessary paperwork and I basically told them that I will next reach to the ombudsman to obtain payment as they keep stalling, I can’t imagine that they were for one second thinking that I would rate then more than one star (0 star not being an option). So now you know, ABC HealthCare.

Titles: I went into hospital for an operation…
Review: I went into hospital for an operation and ABC HealthCare covered it all 2 nights stay and the staff in the hospital were amazing.

I went into hospital to have my appendix out and instead of calling to claim on my ABC HealthCare plan I just went through the NHS. Following 4 nights in hospital I sent ABC HealthCare the request to claim on the overnight stay. It took them nearly 2 months to actually send me the claim response. I told them the day I returned home and sent all the required paperwork. This is POOR. I made several enquiries on the app where I was provided different timescales that best suited the person on the other end. They clearly couldn't be bothered to actually check anything, I'd just stop providing this service as its a waste of your money and a waste of everyone's time. I then called up which there is no option to get an update on a claim, got through to a helpful lady who explained what was going on although I feel what was happening was a long drawn out process. Got ALOT of work to do around ease of use and customer care in my opinion.
I went to a ABC HealthCare dental clinic and the insurance experience was seamless.

Titles: I would like to thank ABC HealthCare adviser…
Review: I would like to thank ABC HealthCare adviser Yasin Karmadi for answering all our questions in great detail, as well as involving translators so we could get accurate information, and overall organising a very difficult process for our benefit. We are very thankful and we would like to praise Mr Karmadi for his professionalism and dedication.

Titles: THEY DONT REIMBURSE YOUR COSTS               Suffered a cracked back tooth while on…
Review: I'll start with dental!   Suffered a cracked back tooth while on business travel. Tooth cracked in half and I swallowed the broken half.  Arrived home in UK in some discomfort with cracked tooth cutting the inside of my mouth when eating. I have ABC HealthCare dental through work, so went straight to nearest ABC HealthCare dentist, which to be fair was a spot on service.  Initial exam, followed by core build up and crown, and then hygienist came to 1317GBP.  ABC HealthCare then only reimburse 345GBP? So 972 GBP paid by me?  What would this treatment have cost if I'd just gone NHS?  Considering what has been paid by my company for ABC HealthCare Medical and Dental cover and what I pay in "benefit in kind" tax at the highest rate over the last 18 years, this is pretty poor.  On the medical side.  I tested positive for Lyme disease last year. Guess what treatment is specifically excluded by ABC HealthCare? Given the increase in Lyme disease in the UK........  I have had 2 recent consultants appointments where the consultant has not even reviewed my CT scan or blood test results, but just diagnoses Long COVID?? Not bad for 20 mins work for 130GBP! Then ABC HealthCare tried to bill me for one of the appointments!  Wife was diagnosed by optician with cataract in one eye and referred to ABC HealthCare. Traveled 300 mile round trip to see consultant recommended by ABC HealthCare, to discover that he no longer did that type of surgery! No apology, explanation or compensation from ABC HealthCare?  More recently my wife just saw another consultant who informs us that due to the cataract being "high risk" ABC HealthCare dont cover the costs of this procedure.....but we can just pay ourselves or wait for a couple of years on NHS, even though it will be the same consultant that carries out the procedure.  Considering what has been paid by my company for ABC HealthCare Medical and Dental cover and what I pay in "benefit in kind" tax at 46% over the last 18 years, this is pretty poor and I would not recommend this service.

I've been using ABC HealthCare for good few years (work provided insurance) and so far had only good experience. Always pleasant and helpful. Any issues with authorisation codes I had were solved within minutes. I am always provided with complete instructions to make my experience as smooth as possible.

I've been waiting for 45 minutes and have tried two different phone numbers to speak to someone at ABC HealthCare and still no answer. Tried the online chat and it's not active despite this being their opening hours. Unfortunately, my employer switched to ABC HealthCare last month, so I have no choice. I'm simply trying to set up my account because doing so on the website doesn't work. If this is how hard it is just to activate my account, I'm very concerned that i will have to dedicate hours to phone calls every time I need care.  We used to have Cigna, and this was NEVER an issue.

If you have a claim at the end of the year - you will be charged the excess and a week later another excess - because it’s a new year.   (£500 + £500).    For the same cancer claim - this seems very unfair.

Impossible to receive treatment. Tried to receive treatment for a mental health condition and they have refused treatment based one thing vaguely similar being in my medical history from 15 years ago.  Their policy says something is pre existing if you’ve had treatment within 5 years. I’ve had to wait weeks for my GP to fill in the form (and pay them) to say I’ve had no treatment at all in the last 15 years but now ABC HealthCare are asking for more info from GP. To prove wellness in last 5 years, even though there are no related records in my history in the last 5 years!? So they don’t trust or even understand their own pre treatment form!  Basically there trying everything they can to try find a loophole to withstand treatment for me.   This is the worst company I’ve ever experienced with in dealing with private medical care.  If I had known how poor their service was I would never have signed up with them.  Please please please do yourself a favor and choose a different provider.  *Edit: ABC HealthCare underwriters are the lowest of the low.  Their policy is that it’s not pre existing if you’ve had no treatment within 5 years. I HAVE NEVER RECEIVED TREATMENT IN MY LIFE, ONLY THING RELATABLE IS FROM 2008 WHEN I DIDNT RECEIVE TREATMENT. ABC HealthCare: “but how do we know that your condition isn’t ongoing and you have actually been receiving treatment in the last 5 years.. you need to prove that you’ve been well since 2008” Me: “but there’s nothing in my medical history to say I have received treatment ever!” ABC HealthCare: “we need letter from your doctor to prove you’ve been well in the last 5 years” Me: “but if I’ve been unwell and received treatment in the last 5 years it would be in my medical history and on the form” ABC HealthCare: “but how do we know you’ve not received treatment..”  And round and round we go..  So I’m guilty until proven innocent. How can I prove that a condition isn’t pre existing other than not having it in my medical records??  So any condition that I try to claim treatment for they can say, although it’s not in your medical records you need to prove you’ve been well in the last 5 years..   How do I prove wellness? Your doctor needs to say you’ve been well. Medical records show no treatment. Not enough. What?!!  ABC HealthCare are a joke of a company. I will soon to be cancelling my policy and advising my company to switch all employees to an alternate provider.  The extra stress of trying to legitimately claim treatment for a mental health condition has caused me I would of definitely just gone to my GP instead, which I will end up doing after yet more weeks and months of back and forth and waiting for the complaints department etc.  Bottom line is they want me to pay money for private healthcare that is impossible to use, so what’s the point..

Titles: In my experience I have found ABC HealthCare hard…
Review: In my experience I have found ABC HealthCare hard to get to fund anything minor (they don't cover a lot of minor things), and anything really major they pass on to NHS.  If this insurance wasn't covered by my work I would not consider this product value for money.

Titles: General inconsistent and poor customer advice/support
Review: Inconsistent information. Also very difficult to have a continues conversation with multiple different people. Promised call backs never happened.
Input dental receipt and received prompt payment with no hassle.

Titles: Insurance claim process handled by my…
Review: Insurance claim process handled by my dentist which was perfect!
It has become almost impossible to contact ABC HealthCare by phone without having to hold on the phone for at least an hour. This is unacceptable.

Titles: It is a shame that ABC HealthCare has basically…
Review: It is a shame that ABC HealthCare has basically outsourced all of its car to other providers and you have to all of the arranging yourself. I have been with other providers such as AXA PPP and found them to be far superior.   It is nearly impossible to contact ABC HealthCare on the phone, they have made this extremely hard. From speaking to the healthcare providers, this is also the same for them - just cannot get through to them.

Titles: It is quick and easy to put the claim…
Review: It is quick and easy to put the claim in and my case was processed within a few days. Very handy to be able to tick the box to receive an updates on your case via a text message. Really pleased with everything.

Titles: It is taking charge information and…
Review: It is taking charge information and informing me with what part I must pay. Done quickly and sensibly

Titles: It is very unclear how to pay
Review: It is very unclear how to pay
It seems like they specifically attempt for you to not be able to use the insurance by being unable to get through on the phone. Over 2 hours on hold unless you call at 8am on the dot.
It simply doesn't work
It takes far too long to get through on the telephone.  Once you get through, the service is generally very good.

Titles: It takes on average 45 minutes to get…
Review: It takes on average 45 minutes to get through

Titles: It took 3 days for me to actually get…
Review: It took 3 days for me to actually get through 😡 When I did the service was good

Titles: It took 3 weeks of constant phoning and…
Review: It took 3 weeks of constant phoning and 10 days from internet contact to get to speak to anyone. I then got an authorisation code to proceed. after my visit to the specialist I was told they couldn't pay the bill without further information, even though I had the authorisation code. I tried in vane to speak to someone to find out what they needed. Eventually I went back to the complaints department and even they said they did not know what was going on. ABC HealthCare are an absolute shower who never!!!! answer the phone.

Titles: It took me 3 very long attempts over…
Review: It took me 3 very long attempts over three days to get a representative to answer the phone. Private medical insurance is not cheap and I expect a far better service than I received. Once I had my call eventually answered it all went smoothly. It was getting very close to my appointments and was very stressful trying to get an authorisation code. You don't need this when you are unwell.

Titles: It took me a total of 4 hours to…
Review: It took me a total of 4 hours to eventually get through to ABC HealthCare for pre auth I was quarter 45 mins the first day second day and gave up both days as my phone charge went Disgraceful when I am paying almost 1000 per month for my husband and I  Clearly working from home is not working

Titles: It was a breeze to deal with anyone…
Review: It was a breeze to deal with anyone involved

Titles: It was a very easy process
Review: It was a very easy process and I found a suitable provider (physio) within the day.

Titles: It was easy (although a bit time…
Review: It was easy (although a bit time consuming) and I got friendly service. Using the online referral system means no visit to my GP.

Titles: Easy online claims process, fast payment
Review: It was extremely easy to claim for dental treatment online, and the reimbursement was extremely fast – excellent

Titles: It was great to know that medical help…
Review: It was great to know that medical help was there when you need it most. The claims process was easy with staff being very friendly and helpful.

Titles: It was great very quickly dealt with…
Review: It was great very quickly dealt with and people were lovely

Titles: It was quick and easy to make the cliam.
Review: It was quick and easy to make the cliam.

Titles: It was quick enough compared to NHS…
Review: It was quick enough compared to NHS long waiting lists.
It was straightforward obtaining an authorisation code which makes such a difference when you are feeling unwell.
It was straightforward to speak to an agent by phone, and they made every effort to ensure our coverage was appropriate and in order.  Our thanks to Holly, Chelsea and Georgia.
It was very easy to arrange and handled very professionally.

Titles: It was very quick and easy
Review: It was very quick and easy. All the staff that o spoke with were very helpful and lovely. They helped me sort everything out

Titles: Starting to go a bit downhill...
Review: It would be unfair to give the lowest rating but I definitely feel I need to alert ABC HealthCare management, because things are worsening. I've been a member for 5 years and I benefited from their services so many times as a happy customer. Recently, however, the customer service quality has worsened, and I see from other reviews that generally speaking waiting times have gone up. Today in particular using the webchat I was bounced from team to team for 45 mins to then get disconnected. I had to start over and after 1h15 mins in total I was told that my claim would take 21 working days to be processed. I was used to get premium service from ABC HealthCare, now it feels like contacting a phone network provider! Please get it right before it goes really wrong.

It's a classic situation. Significant stress as initially reimbursement of overpayment of insurance premium took months to receive, after numerous chasers by me!. Then received acknowledgement of their systems and an apology by ABC HealthCare that they process was not acceptable and a compensation payment was due to me. However progressing this compensation payment has been equally cumbersome. Paperwork processing was around two weeks for their approval, then it was 'cheque in post' for nearly two weeks and still NOT received. I have their medical insurance and, as a pensioner, it might warrant use to overcome severe stress for a pensioner. Finally received cheque reimbursement - 22nd May ABC HealthCare letter accepting their service was inadequate and compensation was due to me and 12th June when cheque finally arrived in the post, approx 3+ weeks later!
It’s been 4 weeks since my first appointment to which I require treatment and I am still waiting to hear any update regarding getting an appointment.
It’s been an amazing experience
It’s been simple to engage with you and the service has been prompt. I’ve appreciated the clarity of the phone we calls with your staff who have all been compassionate and as helpful as possible.

I’m in to my second year of membership with ABC HealthCare and have just tried to use them for a bad shoulder. It’s been a nightmare and very expensive. Having spent £3.5K in monthly fees I’ve been handed a hefty bill. It’s impossible trying to get hold of them on the phone, your left waiting up to 45mins to get an answer, the best was 15. Eventually I get an authorisation number and 3 specialist which they recommended and then you have to choose and book yourself. Out of the 3 only 1 was available so that’s who I selected. Had my appointment and the specialist advised an injection was the remedy and I can do it now. So I agreed and thought that would be it. I have today received 2 bills. 1 for the specialist which I have to pay an excess which I was aware off. The second is for the centre where I had the procedure which is £365, ouch. I’ve been told by ABC HealthCare again after waiting ages the centre wasn’t in their network of hospitals so they wouldn’t cover it!! What how was I meant to know that and in fact why should I they recommended the specialist so I would thought they would only recommend ones that work in their network I’m flabbergasted. So what should have been a fairly simple procedure has cost £615 and it would appear I need a 2nd jab which the specialist did advise at the time. It’s back to the NHS so it’s going to be a long wait but I can’t justify the cost of the appalling service by ABC HealthCare. Shop around or my wife and I are going to start saving the monthly fees which we can use if required. Clive Bayliss The saga continues I have just heard from my complaint handler, even though they have admitted I was not informed the place where I was seen is not in their network. The good news is they would cover those costs but the bad news is now I’m cancelling the policy they won’t!! How can this be right the monthly payments go out on the 7th of the month, June’s was paid and I cancelled the direct debit yesterday 29th June and as you have to do informed the company which I did on the 29th and they’ve cancelled the policy  immediately rather than the 6th of July which is when we are paid up to. Be very if you’re thinking of getting health cover and don’t use ABC HealthCare. It has been a very expensive mistake. A bit of good news they have admitted their mistake and will now pay the Candover clinic bill which is a great relief even though my shoulder still hurts. All in all it’s not been a good experience be sure to read the small print!!

Titles: I’m so impressed with how attentive and…
Review: I’m so impressed with how attentive and caring the staff are at ABC HealthCare. It’s easy to call them up and I always get a timely response.

Titles: Incompetent member support and poor communications
Review: I’ve been with ABC HealthCare for over fifteen years. Consistently their telephone service has been poor with long delays, and I recently had the worst experience ever. I received my annual renewal and the cost of our family of three had risen from approximately £11,000 to £17,000. I called them to ask if we could trim some elements from the cover to reduce the cost and the telephone assistant re-quoted for a reduced cover package, but instead of a reduction she quoted a further increase to over £18,000. I found the person to be completely incompetent and lacking in product knowledge or qualified to advise. She seemed to be merely following prompts on a computer screen. I asked to register a complaint and was informed that I needed to stay on the line longer in order for a complaint process to be instigated.  After spending 50 minutes on the call I  decided to shop around and have since transferred to AXA who I found to be super-efficient and I now pay less than £5,000 for the same cover. I am habitually resistant to change but wish I’d taken the time years ago to shop around for a better provider!

I’ve had a few dealings with Tima from the Cash Plan team, and I have to say I have been astounded with the level of customer service demonstrated and her willingness to go the extra mile, she evidently cares for ABC HealthCare customers and is a huge asset to your organisation.

I’ve just signed up for private health insurance and had my introductory call with Healthcare Consultant, Jake Singh. It was quite possibly one of the best calls I’ve ever had! Jake was incredibly patient and thoroughly informative and took the time to explain the areas I was less knowledgeable on. Nothing was too much of an issue and I felt truly cared for on a one to one basis. I wasn’t rushed off the phone and I was made to feel like a valued customer. Jake was very friendly and had a very kind nature about him. He was also incredibly professional and had extensive knowledge on company policy which was very helpful. I truly enjoyed my call with him! Exceptional customer service. Thank you
I’ve recently had cardiac surgery and am now having a follow up consultation. The young man at ABC HealthCare was very pleasant, professional and efficient. I couldn’t ask for more.

Titles: I’ve used ABC HealthCare twice now
Review: I’ve used ABC HealthCare twice now, it’s definitely worth paying for their service. Professional and helpful customer service, and great variety of choice when choosing a consultant. Most importantly, a GP on hand at all times via the app.  Unfortunately with the way the NHS is going this is a great way of making sure you are as healthy as can be, as that’s the most important thing.

Jake was very patient with me and answered my questions clearly. I didn’t feel rushed to make a decision on the spot and was the time to make an informed choice on what plan is right for me. Keep up the good work Jake.
Knowledgeable, kind and very helpful

Titles: Lack of response to request on 3…
Review: Lack of response to request on 3 occasions. No communication pre the decision not to pay until i stayed on a call for 20 mins . The extraction i had and subsequent treatment were 2 separate sessions, only once id had the extraction did i sign up for the further treatment.

Last night, I experienced a medication reaction that was given to treat stomach ulcers as a result of an endoscopy. I couldn't sleep at all and wanted to consult a digital GP at 2 AM. Unfortunately, the ABC HealthCare blua app didn't provide suitable options in the symptom checker for me to book an appointment and ask me to self treat. Babylon's symptom checker was much better then this new app. Eventually, I had to resort to using the 111 service and waited an hour to speak with an NHS GP. I plan to provide this feedback to our company's HR as there's no point in having insurance if it can't be effectively utilized. This app needs to have more flexibility for a person to able to write their symptoms or choose other and be able to still book in case the symptom checker doesn’t have options.

Titles: Let me down when I needed them most.
Review: Let me down when I needed them most.  Changed my employer who provided ABC HealthCare cover,  whilst undergoing treatment, and took out private continuation cover, which they then refused the treatment on.  Feel cheated and they have let me down when I needed them most.  Despite having ABC HealthCare cover throughout my treatment period, they can't even decide amongst themselves the actual reason for declining, I have gone from them providing a pre-authorization, to having it put on hold, to having it declined for 2 different reasons.  I wouldn't recommend, they are very keen to take the premiums, but not provide the payouts.  Will be escalating this as a formal complaint to the ombudsman.

Like most big companies nowadays , you take out cover then cant get through to them any more to change something on your policy , I got a email telling me to ring a number because she's unable to sort it from her end/which was invalid,  I finally get through and she wants to get the said colleague to ring me back to sort it ,I said wowww slow down, she says well I don't know why she's advised that ?? , as they say in Scotland, "absolute Passhe " Think its best to take out cover with a couple of providers so you can see who's worth it and cancel the other , Great Britain, where everything is broken .pffff game on , help :(((

Titles: Long wait for phone to be answered
Review: Long wait for phone to be answered - also had an instance where code wasn't recognise by AI and payment rejected - took two attempts of over an hour each to resolve situation.  May have got the date wrong - i have just guessed.
Lovely staff, good time keeping
MADE TO FEEL YOU WHERE WELCOME AND EVERYONE WAS ON THE BALL COMING IN CHECKING ALL WAS OK AND CARRYING OUT TESTS ETC VERY PROFESSIONAL

Titles: Messed up last 2 invoices for follow up…
Review: Messed up last 2 invoices for follow up consultations, treating them as initial consultations so for me to pay.  This shouldn't have happened as they were clearly follow up appointments after my day surgery.  Had to contact ABC HealthCare both times to correct it.  I used the online chat to get this sorted.

Moratorium period essentially makes my policy useless to me for 2 years. Trying to speak to them on phone is as bad as trying to call my GP, endless call wait times and no customer service. I have an open complaint with them right now that I've had zero response on for nearly 3 weeks and instead I got this trustpilot invite to review them! If you want to use the policy for anything chronic or mental health related you may as well go the NHS route.

Titles: Most staff at ABC HealthCare were very helpful
Review: Most staff at ABC HealthCare were very helpful

Multiple calls taken after more than 1 hour wait.  They keep promising to pay the physio after an already approved therapy, but still not progressing anything.  Waiting more than 4 weeks for the payment to be processed. Worst service ever!

Titles: My advisor called my dentist to verify…
Review: My advisor called my dentist to verify my treatment as I had not supplied an itemised bill.  Very impressed.

Titles: My appointment was cancelled and I was…
Review: My appointment was cancelled and I was promised a call back with a rescheduled date which has not happened

My appointment was made for a convenient time and took place punctually and efficiently, with excellent levels of care throughout. Only complaint would be the time (+40mins) taken on the phone to ABC HealthCare to get hold of an authorization code for the procedure.

My call to Ashleigh, the ABC HealthCare Agent was so very helpful and kind whilst being very professional- my reason for calling was dealt with efficiently and satisfactorily- thank you ABC HealthCare
My call was answered quickly and  my query dealt with politely and professionally
My call was answered quickly and triage arranged promptly. I was given 3 local option for my physio to arrange directly. Unfortunately one was very local but this maybe an systems fault.

Titles: Don’t expect to be able to see your choice of medical professional.
Review: My choice of physiotherapist has been removed from ABC HealthCare’s list (because she didn’t see enough patients in our rural community) and ABC HealthCare refuse to reimburse  me if I see her, despite being closest to where I live.

My company moved over to ABC HealthCare for dental insurance this year. I've had endless problems with them, their ABC HealthCare touch portal is full of bugs, and I've been unable to raise a claim since joining. I've logged a ticket with their digital team after spending more than 40 min waiting to talk to someone. It's been 4 days since I've logged the issue and it is not yet resolved, and they do not respond to emails. I would avoid joining ABC HealthCare at all cost... I wish my company did not decide to move to ABC HealthCare. We were previously with Denplan, Simplyhealth, and their service was excellent. AVOID!!

Titles: My company recently changed over from…CIGNA
Review: My company recently changed over from Cigna (excellent service) to ABC HealthCare. First called on the 15th June 2023 and spoke to Carl who gave me a pre authorisation number for an upcoming consultation. he was extremely efficient. However, on the 18th July at 12.08 I called to get pre authorisation codes for scans my consultant has booked for October and December and the lady I spoke to was not helpful at all. I had to repeat myself multiple times as she didn't appear to understand me. (she had a French accent) I was distressed as she said she couldn't authorise the scans as they were too far ahead and said that I also may not be eligible. I have raised this with my HR department to investigate further.

Titles: My enquiry was handled well with no…
Review: My enquiry was handled well with no problems.

My experience at the practice was brilliant but booking in as a new patient was not the best experience and if I didn't have dental insurance with ABC HealthCare, I might have given up. The dentist was really good, gave clear explanations of what was needed and a comprehensive plan of action.

My experience has been nothing short of poor.  Can never speak to anyone.  Average online wait time to chat to someone has been 70+ minutes.  Can't get through on the phone.  Been waiting over a month to hear back on a claim I have submitted.

Titles: Paying a premium for the privilege of being inconvenienced
Review: My experience is not a good one and I feel ABC HealthCare as a brand should be much more conscientious of its approach to dealing with the health of its customers.  The general impression I get from the extensive telephone discussions I have held with multiple individuals, is that ABC HealthCare appears to want to do everything within its power to make your experience a horrific one. The process feels more like an attempt to wear down the customer by way of attrition if nothing else.  I have been swapped and passed off to various call handlers on numerous occasions, then told they cannot get through to team xyz and so will call me back later or tomorrow. I am sorry, but if I had cancer for example and was being chucked around as I have been, then I would absolutely have a mental collapse.  ABC HealthCare at this point are more likely to cause me health issues, as opposed to resolving them. ABC HealthCare should perhaps realise that we pay our premiums so that we can be helped swiftly and efficiently. Why is there an endeavour and/or active campaign by ABC HealthCare to make everything so difficult?   It is absolutely shameful in my opinion and ABC HealthCare should be embarrassed. I would not recommend ABC HealthCare. I am in the process of sourcing an alternative health care provider.  The responses from ABC HealthCare on here to those with complaints and concerns are even more shameful. You can see the lack of interest in retaining or helping customers. It is an embarrassment and a money scam.

Titles: My experience with ABC HealthCare is so great and…
Review: My experience with ABC HealthCare is so great and seamless. The advisor is polite and knowledgeable. I got admitted to a reputable hospital in London, and all staff are lovely. Me getting private healthcare is life-changing.

Titles: My experience with ABC HealthCare has been…
Review: My experience with ABC HealthCare has been extremely frustrating. I spent several hours attempting to book an appointment without success. The app crashed repeatedly, and I had to call various teams to address the issue. I paid an outrageous amount of money, for myself and my son, yet the quality of service I received was comparable to the NHS, if not worse. The fact that I had to pay for this subpar service only added to my frustration. I am very disappointed and saddened by the whole experience.

Titles: My queries were dealt with efficiently…Efficient
Review: My queries were dealt with efficiently and I valued being in ABC HealthCare

Titles: My query was dealt with quickly and…
Review: My query was dealt with quickly and efficiently.

Titles: My visit to ABC HealthCare was great

My wife and I  have been with ABC HealthCare for at least four decades and have aways four their telephone representatives to be friendly, efficient, professional and extremely helpful.  We have found that their administration and accounting efficient too.  We heartily recommend ABC HealthCare.

All the Doctors, nurses and support staff worked well together and had an excellent rapport, i had a successful treatment, for which I'm extremely grateful.  The hospital is maintained to a high standard and i thoroughly enjoyed the food.

Titles: Not too long a wait til representative…

Obtaining the authorisation code for my procedure was very easy. Paying the excess was easy. Unfortunately, the procedure went wrong, so I will need it done at a later time and the lady I spoke with on the phone about this was very reassuring that it would still be covered and she provided me with all the information I needed for the future. She was also very sympathetic, which I appreciated very much.
Ok - would be good to have a follow email to ask all the questions you forget to ask in consultation

Titles: Omar and Ged made a huge difference to…
Review: Omar and Ged made a huge difference to supporting and helping me with my questions  Honestly corporate ABC HealthCare is phenomenal, private is terrible and I wouldn't recommend

Titles: On the whole a good service
Review: On the whole a good service.  Unfortunately in recent times, getting through a newly automated call-handling service can be annoying to impossible. Once through that system call wait times can be very long listening to music, always seem to be busy.  After the wait, once with an agent I typically find them friendly and helpful and advise on the best routes of treatment or consultants.

On the whole the people on the phone are very nice and polite but there are a few things that are really annoying and it would make a huge difference to the service and experience if they could be addressed and improved. Firstly, ABC HealthCare ask me to keep calling to update them on everything to do with my claim - such as where the newly requested X-rays will be done, which is fine except that a) I often have to wait some time to get through on the phone which is frustrating in itself. If there was a separate number or button to press for following up on an existing claim that would be helpful and probably speed thing up! And b) when I do speak to someone at ABC HealthCare to update my claim (eg. If the consultant has said he wants X-rays done in addition to the mri requested), each time I speak to someone new who doesn’t know anything about my claim and I have to go through all the details again about who the specialist I am seeing is and where I am seeing them and what it’s about. Surely notes could be kept up-to-date on the system so I don’t keep having to go through it all and keep having to get new authorisation codes with every new update!

Titles: Once I had an authorisation for treatment
Review: Once I had an authorisation for treatment everything worked extemely well.  Getting an authorisation for treatment was a nightmare withlong telephone delays and equally long delays in trying to have a chat.  I hope they have sorted our the inability of members to make contact with only a reasonable waiting time

Once I had managed to get authorisation for consultation and subsequent operation it was fine. However I tried to phone for authorisation mid afternoon on 2 weekdays and held on to "music" for 38 and 27 minutes with no reply. Tried on line and the "chat" showed so typed in my question and it came back there was a 58 minute delay. As it was cancer related I was able to seek authorisation on line but still waited 3 days to receive it. This is unacceptable for  members who are already stressed at this time.

Titles: Long wait for the call to be answered but good service once I spoke to someone
Review: Once I spoke to someone they were helpful but I had to wait 45 minutes for one call and over an hours for the next. Very frustrating and no timescales given whilst on the call about how long the wait was and no option for a callback.

Once again, staggeringly long waiting times to get through to authorise treatment. I tried using the app - but that is no better. Just the usual, pointless bot, which provides no help. How does a company get way with treating its customers like this?
Once the process started it flowed smoothly
Once you get through no issues , really helpful

Titles: Once you get through to someone
Review: Once you get through to someone, they are always informed, helpful and efficient. It does take an extraordinary long time to have your call picked up - approx. 1+hour on hold is a trial! Other than the long delay getting connected this would be a 5 star experience

Titles: One of the best services I have ever…
Review: One of the best services I have ever used! Holly I spoke too made my experience so easy and effortless, potentially saving my life! So I am very grateful to have this experience

Review: One of the worst and pathetic insurance service providers ever seen. I have a corporate insurance and whenever I try to use that I never get any useful response and guidance I feel it's just a waste of money I pay for insurance when you can't access the same.
